======================================================================

“Warden, your dog is lecturing me again.”
Character
Ariane
Title
Hunter
Race
Elf
Gender
Female
Class
Warrior
Specialization
Champion
Affiliation
The Warden-Commander
Location
Korcari Wilds
Quests
In Search of Morrigan
Voice
Corri English
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
-
Witch Hunt
Heroes of Dragon Age
Ariane
is a
Dalish elven
warrior
and one of three
companions
for
the Warden-Commander
in the
Witch Hunt
DLC
for
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Background
Ariane is a gifted Dalish warrior, and her clan's best hunter. When an ancient artifact belonging to the clan's
Keeper
, Solan, is stolen, one with a history tied to the very roots of the elven people, Ariane is charged with the task of reclaiming it. She is determined to carry out her solemn duty, and when her task turns out to intersect with the Warden-Commander's quest to find
Morrigan
she realizes that there may be far more to the artifact's disappearance than simple theft.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Witch Hunt
.
Ariane is first encountered at
Flemeth
's empty hut in the
Korcari Wilds
. She reveals she was sent there by her
Keeper
to locate
Asha'bellanar
. Ariane was hoping to secure her aid in locating her daughter, Morrigan, who had stolen an ancient text related to the
Eluvians
which Ariane's clan had guarded since the days of
Arlathan
. Ariane suggests visiting the
Circle of Magi
for further information since the text was reclaimed from the Circle by an Elf who later joined the Dalish. While researching the Eluvian, the duo are joined by
Finn
, a Circle Mage.
Ariane also offers her blood for the spell to locate the Lights of Arlathan since only pure Dalish blood would work. If the Warden-Commander is also Dalish, their blood won't work because it bears the
Taint
. If a save from
Dragon Age: Origins
was imported, Ariane will also inquire about the ring Morrigan gifted to
the Warden
and the precise nature of their relationship.
Although she initially threatens
Dog
on her first encounter with the Warden, Ariane becomes quite friendly with the mabari and claims she is able to understand Dog's barking.
After arriving at
the Nest
, Ariane asks the Warden-Commander to ask Morrigan about the book before waiting behind with Finn. If Morrigan is allowed to leave but is not followed she will confirm that the Dalish book has been left at her nearby campfire. After the party sees the book and the Warden-Commander picks up the book, the party then proceeds to leave together.
The plot follows BioWare's own canon
, meaning it may not follow some of the player's choices in the games.
After accomplishing their mission, Ariane continued to travel with
Finn
. Eventually some mercenaries hired by Finn's father tracked them down but were driven off.
Finn later returned to his parents' house to explain himself and say goodbye, bringing Ariane along for the visit. She managed to sample every item in the house pantry.
Quests
In Search of Morrigan

“Protect what matters with everything you have, or you'll have nothing, and deserve it.”
Character
Aveline Vallen
Title
Soldier
Guardswoman
Captain of the Guard

of Kirkwall
(c. 9:52 Dragon)
Race
Human
Gender
Female
Class

Kirkwall City Guard
Hawke

Viscount's Keep - Barracks
,
Hightown
Quests
Dragon Age II:
A Friend in the Guard

Hard in Hightown: A Dagger in the Back

Benoit du Lac
(father)
Wesley Vallen
(late husband)
Donnic Hendyr
(husband, conditional)

Joanna Roth
Appearances
Dragon Age II
Heroes of Dragon Age
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
(mentioned)
Aveline Vallen
(née
du Lac
, potentially
Hendyr
) is a human
warrior
. She was fleeing
Ostagar
with her husband
Ser Wesley
during the
Fifth Blight
when they were set upon by
darkspawn
and rescued by
Hawke
and their
family
. Aveline is the first
companion
Hawke
encounters besides their siblings.
Background
See also:
Aveline (short story)
"Aveline Vallen is a soldier, a master of sword and shield, and a tireless guardian... to a point. The daughter of an exiled chevalier, Aveline is not the knight her father wished her to be. Although fully trained in chivalrous combat, she was raised in the shadow of a lost life and will not lose another for the sake of honor. Protecting her adopted home of Kirkwall becomes a chosen duty, not a privileged calling. The people she stands for will not be wasted on lost causes or protecting the foolish from themselves. Pride can be bandaged like any other wound—when threats are dead and everyone is safely home."
Aveline is the daughter of a
chevalier
named
Benoit du Lac
, who fled from
Orlais
to
Ferelden
after losing his patron to assassination. Raising her on stories of knights and adventures, he hoped Aveline would be a knight, even naming her after
Ser Aveline, the Knight of Orlais
. He eventually sold everything he had to sponsor her into King
Cailan Theirin
's service.
Aveline met and married Wesley Vallen while in the Fereldan army, where she was an officer. Her company was part of the first charge. However,
Loghain
's betrayal at the
Battle of Ostagar
forced them to flee the darkspawn horde.
She ordered a scattered retreat and told her people to make their way home.
She and Wesley eventually made their way to
Lothering
's outskirts, where they met
Hawke
.
Involvement
Dragon Age II
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age II
.
Hawke saves Aveline and her husband, Ser Wesley, from imminent death while escaping Lothering. When
Flemeth
indicates Wesley will die of the darkspawn taint, either Hawke or Aveline delivers a mercy blow to him. Wesley's death leaves Aveline with few options, and she accompanies the Hawke family to
Kirkwall
.
Upon arriving at Kirkwall, the group finds the guards must be bribed in order for them to enter the city. Hawke's uncle provides contacts who can help them, in exchange for mercenary services from the Hawkes through the next year. With no other options, Aveline assists the Hawkes in entering the city; if Hawke chooses to help
Athenril
in dealing with
Cavril
, the merchant's cheating ways incense Aveline, and she deals with him very effectively.
Act 1
After entering the city, Aveline joins the
Kirkwall City Guard
to make a living. If Hawke aids her in her companion quest,
The Way It Should Be
, Aveline and Hawke will uncover a conspiracy involving
Jeven
, the current Captain of the City Guard, and the
Coterie
. Hawke and Aveline discover that Jeven has been sending guardsmen on dangerous patrols with a satchel of valuable city information in order to sacrifice them to the Coterie so that the thieves could obtain the information with out looking suspicious. When Jeven's scheme is revealed to the authorities, Jeven will be arrested by his own guardsmen and Aveline will replace him as the new Captain of the Guard for her role in ending Jeven's corruption. Aveline will still be the Captain of the Guard, though considered "interim", if Hawke does not aid her in her companion quest since the previous captain left suddenly and Aveline with her previous military experience made her the most viable replacement. Aveline's guard and Guard-Captain positions allows her to deal with certain people effectively, namely other guards and scoundrels.
Act 2
When Hawke
visits
Aveline three years later and her companion quest,
The Way It Should Be
, was completed, she warns Hawke that their new found wealth and influence has changed the fortunes of many people in Kirkwall—and not always for the better. When asked how she was settling into her new position as Guard Captain, Aveline complains that they are spread thin and how the
templars
are unwilling to requisition a few of their men to fight for the city guard as it was considered "demeaning". Even so, many of the guardsmen seem to have rallied under her leadership. If her companion quest was not completed, she states that the previous captain left suddenly, and she now realizes why running the guard is so difficult. The viscount is weak and is unwilling to commit the resources she needs for the city guard. Not only because her position is only "interim", but also because he fears it would upset the templars. If Hawke encourages her to just leave the guard and work for them, she says that she knows the type of trouble Hawke gets into and that a casual relationship with them suits her just fine.
Hawke can also
assist
Aveline in her relationship with Guardsman
Donnic Hendyr
; if helped, she marries him at the Hawke Estate. If not, Aveline remains single.
If brought into the
Fade
during
Night Terrors
, the desire demon will succeed in turning Aveline against Hawke by using her memory of Wesley; after returning from the Fade, Aveline confesses her worries in
Doubts that Linger
.
If brought along, Aveline disapproves of accusing
Grand Cleric Elthina
of wrongdoing in
Offered and Lost
.
Like other companions, Aveline has a gift,
The Shield of the Knight Herself
.  Regardless of what Hawke has done with
Ser Wesley's Shield
, Aveline will complain about having to replace it when she is presented with the new shield. To avoid rivalry points, Hawke must explain that the shield is a gift for a friend or take it back.
In
Prime Suspect
, Aveline requests Hawke's help in silencing Emeric's investigations into a series of murders. Aveline explains that she and the guards followed his leads but were made fools of themselves by the noble Gascard DuPuis, who was the prime suspect of the case. Emeric will later be killed. In
All That Remains
, Hawke's mother Leandra is kidnapped and killed and DuPuis' mentor is revealed to be the serial killer. Aveline will attempt to console Hawke for their mother's death in
The Captain's Condolences
. Hawke can assure her they're fine or confide their sorrow to her for friendship points. In seeking to comfort Hawke, Aveline offers to tell a story about her father. Hawke can also blame Aveline's incompetence for
Leandra
's death for rivalry points. In response, Aveline dismisses the accusation by focusing on Hawke's grief.
In the quest
Demands of The Qun
, Aveline enlists Hawke's aid in trying to arrest two fugitive elves who have been granted religious sanctuary in the
Qunari Compound
for converting to the
Qun
. The elves murdered the guardsman who raped their sister after their complaints were dismissed by the Guards. The
Arishok
takes offense to the attempted arrest of his new converts and counterattacks, marshaling his forces to conquer the city and convert it to the
Qun
.
Act 3
When Hawke
visits
Aveline three years after the Qunari attack, she states that without a viscount, the templars have been trying to influence the city guard more.
Aveline can meet Jeven again in her companion quest,
Favor and Fault
. Since his fall from grace, Jeven has taken up with base types who hold prejudiced views against Fereldans like Aveline and Hawke. He became the leader of a dangerous group of insurgents who attempted to have Captain Aveline expelled by forcing anonymous complaints that she was coddling her men. Jeven even attempted to coax guards into joining his cause. In addition to getting revenge for his arrest at Aveline's hands if Aveline's first companion quest was completed, the scheme was intended to reduce what Jeven saw as excessive Fereldan influence in Kirkwall, though the plan backfired when Captain Aveline confronted him in the
sewers
of Kirkwall. He was killed after attacking Aveline.
Aveline's attitude towards her position as Guard-Captain depends on Hawke's friendship or rivalry status. If Aveline has a friendship status, she is proud of her efforts in reforming the Kirkwall City Guard and is thankful to Hawke for encouraging her. Aveline allows every guard to choose their patrol and promotes from the bold and the cautious. Both the Guard and most of the citizenry approve of Aveline's efforts to turn the Kirkwall City Guard into a force of order and stability for the city. If rivalry, Aveline becomes disillusioned with her efforts as Guard-Captain and is plagued with doubts. In a rivalry relationship, Aveline is stricter and more demanding of the Kirkwall City Guard in order to maintain its reputation as a competent and respectable police institution. Aveline only retains the employment of the guards who are tough enough by her appraisal. Even so, casualties amongst the city guard are down thanks to her leadership. Her high-handed measures and stringent leadership have led to discontent among some of the guards (several of whom benefited from Jeven's corruption and favor), while others believe they need her leadership to keep the Kirkwall City Guard from falling apart. If some of her guardsmen were killed in Jeven's attempted insurrection she may even consider resigning from her position.
During
the Last Straw
, Aveline does not approve of allowing
Anders
to live; if Hawke does, and puts both Aveline and Anders in the party, she will tell him to turn himself in. Aveline always joins Hawke who sided with the templars. In most cases she also immediately joins Hawke who sided with the mages.
However, Aveline initially refuses to support Hawke who sided with the mages if her Act 3 quest
Questioning Beliefs
has not been completed and Hawke has 100% friendship or rivalry with Fenris and has completed his last personal quest. Later Hawke meets Aveline in the Gallows courtyard. Hawke can still convince Aveline to join the party at this point. Otherwise Aveline walks away and then shows up inside the Gallows with a group of city guards. She refuses to fight Hawke in battle because she owes her life to Hawke. Instead, she angrily leaves stating that she wants nothing to do with Hawke anymore.
If Aveline decides to help Hawke side with the mages,
and
has been married to Donnic, Donnic will order the city guard to protect the civilians, keeping them out of the fight. If Aveline
did not
marry Donnic and decides to help the mages, the guards will participate in the fight, and Aveline will blame Hawke for their deaths.
Dragon Age: Inquisition
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The
Inquisitor
can ask
Varric Tethras
about her fate. Varric will state that she is still Guard-Captain and is responsible for a lot of Kirkwall's stability. In a
letter
from
Bran Cavin
, it is revealed that Aveline has formed and marshals the Kirkwall citizens militia to defend the city from occupation by foreign forces.
If Hawke sided with the Templars:
A
speech
given by
Senior Enchanter Lorace
of
Cumberland
given before the
Divine Conclave
describes that Hawke left running of the city to Aveline and Seneschal Bran, and that Aveline refused to speak about Hawke's whereabouts.
If Hawke's sibling has joined the
Grey Wardens
:
Hawke will mention at
Skyhold
's battlements that Aveline helped by taking their sibling away from
Corypheus
' influence in order to be safe.
Sebastian Vael
recruited by Hawke, Anders survived, and the Inquisitor accepts Sebastian's offer of friendship:
Sebastian Vael
reclaims his title as Prince and later leads an invasion of Kirkwall. In the mission
Annexing Kirkwall
, Sebastian requests support from the
Inquisition
in helping him suppress the city's resistance forces led by Guard-Captain Aveline Vallen and hunt down Anders' associates. The
Inquisitor
can choose to send
Leliana
's agents to bolster Sebastian's forces and strong-arm Aveline to back down or send
Cullen
's military to bolster Aveline's resistance forces and repel Sebastian's occupation forces back to
Starkhaven
or, alternatively, not get involved at all.
Provided that the Inquisitor looks into Varric's
issues
that correspond to the
war table
, and opts to send Cullen to deal with the developing problems of her former comrade, Aveline is involved with the
Hard In Hightown
series of war table mission chains. She keeps regular correspondence with members of the Inquisition, sending letters to Varric, and at the completion of the mission chain, provided that Commander Cullen's troops were part of the operation, she sends along a message commenting about the events that have occurred and tells Varric that he owes her.
Trespasser
This section contains spoilers for:
Trespasser
.
In the
Trespasser
DLC
, Aveline is seen in Varric's epilogue picture, smiling, implying she is still the Guard-Captain of Kirkwall.
If the Inquisitor is a Qunari, Bran will mention that Varric giving the guard job to Valo-kas in Kirkwall irritated Aveline so much that she came to his office eleven times to complain.
Note:
Dragon Age Keep
settings indicating that Aveline left Hawke at the end of
Dragon Age II
do not affect any of the aforementioned events.
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
When Varric Tethras left Kirkwall to search for
Solas
, Aveline Vallen was appointed acting Viscount to replace him and assume his duties.
The strange, new
darkspawn
that
Elgar'nan
and
Ghilan'nain
deploy to southern Thedas have spread fear and corruption greater than any previous
Blight
in history. The
Free Marches
suffer greatly from this invasion. Acting Viscount Aveline Vallen leads the evacuation of Kirkwall, taking her people and what's left of her army to help Prince
Sebastian Vael
keep
Starkhaven
.

Friendship and rivalry
See the
approval
page for more information.
Aveline is a kind and moral person who respects authority and values friendship and family. Friendship is earned when Hawke acts lawfully and helps those in need. She is not unreasonable, however, and will occasionally favor bending the law in order to help her friends (especially
Isabela
).
Rivalry with Aveline is earned when Hawke ignores people in need, takes illegal or seedy jobs, or takes jobs solely for personal profit.

- found at Sir
Varnell
's Refuge during the quest
Offered and Lost
.

Codex entries

Shield of the Knight Herself
Trivia
Lukas Kristjanson
wrote Aveline for
Dragon Age II
.
Aveline shows exasperation towards her namesake, the
first female chevalier in Orlais
.
According to Lukas Kristjanson, if Aveline were a food she would be "Steak. Rare. But with caramelized onions and baby corn nibbled from the side like a full cob when no one is looking."
Aveline's favorite color is green.
Aveline has an antagonistic relationship with
Isabela
.
Aveline was at
Ostagar
, and remembers Carver and Hawke (if a warrior or rogue).
Though Aveline was born in
Orlais
, she was raised
Fereldan
and later comes to identify as a Marcher and Kirkwaller.
Although Aveline is not a romance option, she can be flirted with by both a male and female Hawke during
The Way It Should Be
in Act 1 and
The Long Road
during Act 2. If all the flirt options are taken during the latter, Aveline will kiss Hawke in thanks, and then inquire if she and Hawke could have ever had a relationship.
In
Dragon Age: Inquisition
,
should the Inquisitor persuade Varric to write the latest chapter of his romance serial "Swords and Shields" for Cassandra, the cover of the book features an image of a woman greatly resembling Aveline in her Guard Captain gear.
In Dragon Age: Inquisition
, Scout
Harding
at
Skyhold
can say that Varric told her a story about copper marigolds (Aveline's gift to Donnic; see
The Long Road
).
Isabela describes her as "A woman-shaped battering ram"
In her last
Questioning Beliefs
quest, she'll beat Hawke up if they are rivals and antagonize her too much.
In the
Forbidden Oasis
in
Dragon Age: Inquisition
, there is an area called "The DuLac Path". DuLac was the surname of Aveline's father. A note describing one of the landmarks also mentions a letter from one Leon DuLac to his sister, Manon DuLac. It's possible these people are related to Aveline.
Bugs
When using the skill "Immovable", Aveline has a chance of becoming frozen in place in the combat stance, unresponsive to even manual control.  Unlike the skill associated movement reduction, this will not eventually wear off and can only be fixed by leaving the area.
When giving Aveline the
Shield of the Knight Herself
, her dialogue indicates that Ser Wesley's Shield was sold even if it was merely being kept in Hawke's storage chest. (all platforms) However, if you make her equip Wesley's shield and then initiate the dialogue - she will say that she "still has his shield and doesn't need a new one".
During the quest Demands of The Qun, when the Arishok attacks the guards in the compound, Aveline's shield will disappear and her sword will revert to the one she carried when first met for the duration of the scene.

======================================================================
PAGE: Bellara Lutare
CATEGORIES: Characters, Companions, Dragon Age: The Veilguard characters, Dragon Age: The Veilguard companions
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Bellara_Lutare
======================================================================

“The Archive holds parts of my people. Some good. Some bad. And that's the burden. My people's future. On my shoulders.”
Character
Bellara Lutare
Race
Elf
Gender
Female
Class
Mage
Affiliation
Veil Jumpers
Dalish
Veilguard
Location
Arlathan Forest
The
Lighthouse
Quests
In Entropy's Grasp
Family
Cyrian Lutare
(brother)
Voice
Jee Young Han
Appearances
Dragon Age: Vows & Vengeance
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Bellara Lutare
is a
Veil Jumper
and
companion
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
Background
Bellara is described as being plucky, hyper-focused, creative,
and having an obsessive interest in ancient
Elvhenan
and its secrets. She is also creative and romantic with a strong sense of self, having a clear idea of who she is and what she wants, and she is willing to push herself to her limits in her pursuit of finding answers.
Involvement
Dragon Age: Vows & Vengeance
Bellara has stolen an elven artifact from the
Tevinter Imperium
and is on the run from
Imperial templars
in
Arlathan Forest
. Using the dangers of the forest to distract her templar pursuers, Bellara was about to enter a
Fade tear
portal but instead was surprised to find
Nadia Carcosa
and
Drayden Kiel
exiting the portal. Bellara warns them her templar pursuers are out for blood and tells them that they all must flee immediately. Nadia and Drayden follow Bellara but are eventually captured by the templars.
The templar captain demands that Bellara return the artifact she stole but Bellara argues that the Tevinter Imperium are the true thieves, as the Imperial templars come to Arlathan Forest to steal elven artifacts and magic in the Imperium's attempts to control everyone. Bellara proclaims her reclaiming of the artifact was liberation of what was stolen, which resulted in the templar captain and Bellara exchanging threats. Nadia interjects by saying she and Drayden are strangers to Bellara and don't even know what a Veil Jumper is.
Bellara tells Nadia that she needs them to create a distraction so Nadia and Drayden started an annoying argument with each other to distract and lure in the templars. Once properly positioned, Drayden threw at the templars a grenade, which put their jailors in disarray. Bellara then used a magical amulet to temporarily animate some trees into
sylvans
, which distracted the guards long enough for the trio to escape.
Bellara guided Nadia and Drayden in traversing the magical anomalies of the forest, including walking on the water of a river, so they can reach the bubble at the bottom of a waterfall. A bubble is a shimmer, similar to a one-way doorway, that will teleport them to a small area spared from Arlathan's anomalies.
They jump into the bubble, which was a small Fade tear just beneath the surface of the basin water, which teleports them to a less chaotic location. Bellara explained that Veil Jumpers coined the teleportation process as a veil jump.
After reaching safety, Bellara, Drayden and Nadia make formal introductions. Bellara and Drayden seem delighted in their shared love of books, discoveries, and writing, while Nadia is more pushy towards Bellara and tries to steer the conversation to learning details of Veil Jumpers, where they are, and how it can help her quest to rescue her lover
Elio
. Bellara answers Nadia's questions and Nadia becomes interested in Bellara's ability to enter the
Fade
and her work in trying to map it. However, Bellara clarifies that mapping of the Fade is somewhat misleading as once in the Fade, the space is reflective and shifting; the deeper one looks, the less they seem to know about the Fade.
Nadia requests Bellara take them to the Fade and explains her quest to rescue Elio. Bellara questions Nadia if she is certain Elio is still alive as the Fade is a hostile realm. Nadia tells Bellara about the headaches she gets when she's close to a Fade tear and how she can briefly hear Elio calling out to her during such times. After deep consideration, Bellara agrees to help Nadia, and tells her that she too had once lost someone dear to her in an accident involving an artifact gone wrong. Like Nadia, Bellara states she too would have done anything to save them. Bellara uses the elven artifact she had recently stole, called the Flicker, which they plan to use as a guide in the Fade. To power the Flicker, the group ventured back into the anomalies of the forest to a
lyrium
field where the
Veil
is thin. As the group traverses to their destination, Bellara tells Nadia and Drayden that the Veil Jumpers fear the magical chaos found in Arlathan Forest will spread to all of
Thedas
if the Veil Jumpers don't stop the
Dread Wolf
, and that the Dread Wolf had already taken an ancient stone imbued with dangerous magic from the forest. Nadia reveals she's been searching for the Dread Wolf and the group agrees that both their plights seem connected.
Bellara repaired the Flicker but needs to generate a Fade spark. Drayden requests they power the Flicker since they have been Fade-touched ever since they were a child. Lastly, Bellara requests something personal of Elio's to guide the Flicker, and Nadia offered her Elio's great-grandmother's ring. After completing the necessary preparations, the group enters the Fade but they are separated from Nadia.
Bellara and Drayden use the Flicker to find Nadia and defends her from a
demon
in the guise of a templar captain. Bellara attempts to hold off the templar captain, who seems to only grow stronger, while Drayden secures a wounded Nadia. Eventually, the Fade starts to become unstable and begins to manifest a storm. Bellara orders a retreat and tells Drayden to pick up Nadia. The templar captain begins summoning an army of lyrium-corrupted knights from the Fade and orders them to hunt the trio down. The trio escapes from the templar army by running into the Fade storm. The Fade started erecting walls to block their path, so Bellara tells the Flicker to forget the ring and to instead guide them to an escape from the Fade. With Flicker's guidance, the group is led to a fluctuation in the Veil that Bellara used to create a temporary, unstable Fade tear. However, Bellara warns that there is no guarantee any of them will wind up at the same place.
Before they could enter the Fade tear, they are assaulted by the templar captain and his army. Bellara made sure Drayden and Nadia entered the Fade tear first and was about to let the Flicker follow suit, but the templar captain caught the Flicker and destroyed it and took Nadia's ring. This enraged Bellara, who unleashed a severe attack against the templar captain. With the templar captain knocked out, Bellara too escaped into the Fade tear.
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
After interrupting
Solas
's ritual in
Arlathan Forest
and inadvertently trapping him within
the Fade
,
Rook
and their allies return to the forest to investigate. They are attacked by a
sentinel
but rescued by Veil Jumpers
Strife
and
Irelin
who are searching for Bellara. With the help of Rook and their allies, they find Bellara in the forest and learn that they have entered a
Veil Bubble
and will need her assistance to escape. Bellara also notes that the presence of
Darkspawn
in the forest is highly unusual as the
Deep Roads
from which the Darkspawn emerge are not nearby. Bellara helps the group escape the Veil Bubble by locating and activating an artifact known as the
Nadas Dirthalen
(elvhen for "the inevitability of knowledge"). The activation reveals that a crystal within the artifact contains an
Archive Spirit
, but it suddenly vanishes due to the artifact malfunctioning. With the artifact that was drawing in the wild magic deactivated, the Veil Bubble collapses and restores reality. From henceforth, Bellara endeavors to repair the Nadas Dirthalen.
Rook, Bellara, and their allies then return to the Veil Jumper Camp, where they are tasked by Irelin and Strife to check on two Veil Jumpers that were sent to investigate
D'Meta's Crossing
and also to search for any signs that the elven gods may have been there.
The Veilguard discover that
Elgar'nan
and
Ghilan'nain
sacrificed the two Veil Jumpers in a
blood magic
ritual that unleashed the
Blight
on D'Meta's Crossing. The return of the elven gods and the destruction they caused horrified Bellara. As Bellara learns more about ancient elven history and the suffering caused by the
Evanuris
' rule, Bellara begins to worry about how it may impact the perception of modern elves, and also considers if modern elves should even be held responsible for the sins of their ancestors.
In-game, Bellara can use the skill
Tinker
which repairs malfunctioning artifacts. After the quests
In Entropy's Grasp
and
Shadows Crossing
, Bellara joins the
Veilguard
and returns with them to the
Lighthouse
. She uses her expertise with
elven
magic to repair the
Vi'Revas
, the
Eluvian
in the Lighthouse. She later settles in the Lighthouse's workshop where she works on the Nadas Dirthalen.
When Rook joins Bellara in helping the Veil Jumpers make sure their artifacts are secure and calm, Rook notices Bellara is exceedingly strict with the Veil Jumpers' precautious procedures. After helping her secure the artifacts, Bellara tells Rook about her younger brother,
Cyrian
.
Cyrian used to call Bellara Vora'shivan, "hummingbird" in
elven
since she could never sit still. Both Cyrian and Bellara wanted to learn everything about
Elvhenan
—its people, the magic, etc. Cyrian kept Bellara focused and anchored in her pursuits.
The siblings heard about the Nadas Dirthalen and believed it could teach them everything or at least some of what they wanted to know. When Arlathan Forest began to change due to the magical anomalies, Bellara joined the Veil Jumpers to help people. Cyrian unofficially joined as a Veil Jumper ally,
but he kept searching for the Nadas Dirthalen even though his quest kept putting him in danger. During one of the siblings' adventures, an artifact that Bellara took precautions to secure started activating. Bellara couldn't reach her brother in time and he was presumed dead. The Veil Jumpers hailed Cyrian as a hero and offered to make a statue to celebrate him. Ever since then, Bellara has blamed herself for her brother's death. Rook encourages Bellara to overcome her guilt, although she says she has tried. She believes she can only move on by making his death mean something, and the only way she knows how is to honor him via learning about the elven people and finding the truth about their past. Once she accomplishes that, she thinks that her brother will forgive her and she'll feel worthy of it.
Bellara is able to repair the Nadas Dirthalen but it is very condescending and cannot answer any of Bellara's questions unless worded properly. Bellara and Rook discover they need to ask questions condescendingly as well, and when they questioned it about who built the Nadas Dirthalen, they find out its creator was
Anaris
, a
Forgotten One
.
Should Rook speak to Bellara after the
Fall of Weisshaupt
, Bellara received word from
Strife
that Yenarel and other fellow Veil Jumpers have gone missing for a week. Bellara heard the missing Veil Jumpers went back to the Floating Archive Temple where they found the Nadas Dirthalen and she is worried that she caused their peril. Bellara enlists Rook's help in searching for the missing Veil Jumpers, thus starting the quest
The Demon's Bargain
. As the Veilguard tracked the missing Veil Jumpers in the Floating Archive Temple, they discover that it has been re-infested by
demons
and
sentinels
. The Veilguard find two of the missing Veil Jumpers encased in a crystallized enchantment. As Bellara tries to free them, the Veilguard is discovered by Bellara's brother, Cyrian. He is pleasantly surprised to be reunited with his sister but Bellara is shocked that he is still alive. Cyrian reveals he was saved by
Anaris
who he believes will empower the elven people. Anaris reveals himself as a powerful
spirit
who seeks to manifest into physical form with Cyrian's help. Bellara recognizes Anaris as a
Forgotten One
, and Anaris acknowledges that he is part of those forgotten, but not gone. Anaris cryptically says "The sixth and seventh roam free. And the way stands clear." Anaris declares he will guide the elven people to ascension, leading Bellara to deduce that Anaris has been turning the missing elven Veil Jumpers into the demons they've just fought.
Cyrian is surprised that the ritual Anaris taught him could kill his people as he was told it would give the elves strength and purity. Anaris tells Cyrian it was a regrettable possibility that can come about if the ritual is done wrong. Anaris pressures Cyrian to follow through as he insinuates that he lacks the conviction to execute his people's ascension. Bellara asks Cyrian why he follows Anaris, to which Cyrian replies that only Anaris offers the truth and is willing to help the elven people. Bellara rejects improving the elven people this way and regrets seeing what Cyrian has become. Anaris calls Cyrian to follow him, leaving the Veilguard to be attacked by more demons, which they subsequently defeat. Afterwards, the Veilguard free the two bewitched Veil Jumpers, Yenarel and Hamuel. The Veilguard then alert the Veil Jumpers to their location, who then tend to their rescued members. Strife and
Irelin
report that Yenarel and Hamuel are alive. When Bellara mentions that Cyrian was wearing a magical mask that may have been influencing him, Irelin deduces that he was wearing an
Evanuris bond mask
. Strife says they found several Evanuris bond masks but they were all broken or dormant. He suspects that things may have changed with the surge of raw magic that the elven gods brought back into the world. Irelin adds that the mask would link to an elven god or a sufficiently powerful
mage
. It lets the wearer feel whatever their linked master felt and was mostly used to control their servants. Irelin notes that Cyrian isn't being controlled, just feeling whatever Anaris is feeling. Everyone is in agreement that they need stop Cyrian but Bellara thinks she can reason with him.
Back at the Lighthouse, Bellara questions the Nadas Dirthalen in order to understand why Cyrian would work for him. When she asks the Archive Spirit about how Anaris waged war against the Evanuris, the Archive Spirit revealed that he made an army of demons out of the elves of Elvhenan. The answers revealed to Bellara horrified her, and when Rook arrived, she reveals to them that Cyrian wanted to meet to explain himself. Bellara asks Rook to come with her to the meeting and Rook agrees to do so.
During
Hummingbird's Lament
, Bellara brings Rook to the meeting spot in Arlathan Forest—much to Cyrian's surprise. Rook tells Cyrian they don't trust him with Bellara's safety after he helped Anaris turn the missing Veil Jumpers into demons. Cyrian insists that the ritual was meant to give the Veil Jumpers purity and strength, and he blames the ritual's failure on him being unworthy to execute the ritual. Even so, he resolves to do better next time for the sake of Anaris and the elven people. Rook argues that the ritual is horrific and doesn't serve the elves, but Cyrian retorts that Anaris is willing to do whatever he must to save the elves from their dreadful lot; even if he must sacrifice some of them and make hard choices. Cyrian says the
Evanuris
and
Solas
would sacrifice the elves for their own selfish ends but he believes Anaris has a sincere desire to improve the elves. Bellara accuses Anaris of being a devious warlord but Cyrian defends Anaris by saying the Evanuris slandered Anaris—and since they were the victors in the war against the
Forgotten Ones
, they could revise history to demonize him.
Cyrian claims Anaris fought to make the elves great while the Evanuris fought to keep them in fetters. Furthermore, Cyrian says Anaris can make the elves as great as they were during the times of ancient Elvhenan. Bellara tells Cyrian that the caring brother she knew wouldn't condone such callousness for the sake of ambition and accuses Anaris of poisoning his thoughts with the Evanuris Bond Mask. Cyrian insists that he is following Anaris willingly. He states through the mask, he feels what Anaris feels: a genuine hope and happiness when he envisions the elves rising once more. However, Cyrian also reveals that Anaris is afraid of something that he hasn't disclosed to Cyrian yet. This fear unnerves Anaris and consumes his thoughts. Despite this, Cyrian trusts Anaris and feels obligated to help him face this phantom threat. Rook still disapproves of the ritual and Bellara believes the elves deserves better than to be forcefully transformed. Bellara reminds Cyrian that his influence made her self-assured in her morals and actions. Bellara says Cyrian always told her to follow her heart as "it's a good heart and it'll never lie to you." Bellara then asks Cyrian to trust her judgement about Anaris in a last-ditch effort to turn her brother against Anaris. However, he pulls away from Bellara and says he must do what he must. Believing they failed to convince Cyrian, Rook and Bellara resolve to stop the pair.
Back at the Lighthouse, Bellara is researching part of Anaris' artifact that turned the missing Veil Jumpers into demons. Bellara was trying to discover the process of the ritual but the Archive Spirit was no help to her. Bellara expresses her frustration about how she nearly convinced Cyrian to turn away from Anaris and expresses doubt that she can confront her brother. Bellara still has hope that she can reason with Cyrian eventually. Bellara states she has tuned the resonance of the artifact and can use that to find the location of their next ritual so the Veilguard can confront them.
During
Blood of Arlathan
,
Neve Gallus
impersonates a Venatori mage to help the Veilguard infiltrate Elgar'nan's gathering. Elgar'nan used mental domination magic to influence the crowd, tempting them with their desires in exchange for complete obedience to him. However, through the combined efforts of Bellara and Neve's magic, they are able to dispel the hold Elgar'nan had on the Veilguard's minds and continue the mission.
During
Through the Shadows
, the Veilguard track Anaris and Cyrian to Arlathan City—the ancient capital of Elvhenan—in order to stop their next ritual. Anaris' ritual seems to focus on him attaining corporeal form but he also plans to amass an army of demons. The Veilguard infiltrate the Great Hall to destroy a magical
amplifier
that is being used to spread magic throughout the ritual site in order slow the ritual and search for Anaris and Cyrian.
Traversing deeper into the ancient ruins, the Veilguard find and confront Cyrian and Anaris. Though frustrated Bellara followed him, Cyrian nonetheless proceeds with Anaris' ritual, which suddenly blasts Anaris away—revealing Cyrian's sabotage. He removes his Evanuris Bond Mask and tells Bellara he had faith in her judgement and trusted she'd lead him to the right course of action. However, before the group can leave, Anaris emerges in corporeal form from the Ritual Pit. Despite Cyrian's efforts, it was not enough to stop Anaris regaining a physical body. Enraged at the betrayal, Anaris throws Cyrian against a pillar so forcefully that he is fatally injured. The Veilguard rally to fight Anaris and thwart his plans to summon a demon army. Defeated, Anaris cries out that he must escape 'its eye' as his body slowly dematerializes. The Veilguard then rush to Cyrian's side.
Before Cyrian succumbs to his wounds, he and Bellara share their final conversation. Bellara reassures her brother Anaris has been defeated and banished back to the Fade.
He then apologizes to Bellara for the trouble he caused and thanks Rook for helping him and his sister. Bellara stays with Cyrian in his final moments.
Back at the Lighthouse, Bellara reveals that Cyrian left her a final
letter
in the event of his death. With Cyrian gone, Bellara questions if the secrets of the Nadas Dirthalen should be preserved since she thinks it may have led Cyrian to his death. Even so, Bellara acknowledges that the ancient elvhen shaped the world and the knowledge within the Nadas Dirthalen can empower modern elves to do so as well. Yet the ancient elvhen were also responsible for horrific atrocities. Cyrian states in his letter that the knowledge the Archive Spirit has could also become dangerous if it falls into the wrong hands but could also be used to do a lot of good. Rook can encourage Bellara to free the Archive Spirit, losing the knowledge forever but keeping it from ever being misused or, they can encourage Bellara to keep the Archive Spirit's knowledge to help the elven people, though this leaves it at-risk of falling into the wrong hands.
During
The Forest of Spirits
, Bellara holds an elven
funeral
for Cyrian, attended by Rook and some of Bellara's friends. If the Archive Spirit was kept, Cyrian's funeral will be in accordance to the rites performed by the elves of ancient Elvhenan. If it was discarded, then Cyrian's funeral will be in accordance to a version of modern,
Dalish
funerary rites.
During
Isle of the Gods
, the Veilguard fought their way to elven gods' ritual site but was hindered by the gods'
blood magic
wards, with either Bellara or
Neve Gallus
having to risk their lives to dispel them. If Bellara was chosen to dispel the wards, she will be abducted by Elgar'nan and taken through an eluvian. If Neve was chosen to dispel the wards, Bellara survives the quest, escaping the island with the rest of the Veilguard (aside from Rook) before it is destroyed by Elgar'nan after Ghilan'nain's death.
If Bellara didn't dispel the wards during
Isle of the Gods
:
Bellara participates in the
Battle of Minrathous
. Bellara can die during
The Last Gambit
or during
The Dread Wolf Rises
depending on player choices.
If Bellara dispelled the wards during
Isle of the Gods
:
During the
Battle of Minrathous
, Rook and Solas form an uneasy alliance to defeat Elgar'nan. In order for them to reach the Divine's Manor and climb the giant tendril tethered to the Archon's Palace, they must defeat the intelligence controlling the Blight on Elgar'nan's behalf. Rook discovers this intelligence is Bellara, who Elgar'nan has infected with the
taint
and magically compelled to control the Blight. Rook tries to rescue their mage companion but
Lusacan
intervenes, forcing Solas to use his magic to telekinetically throw about the blight tendril and Lusacan. Solas willingly gives Rook his lyrium dagger and tells Rook that he must deliver the final blow to Elgar'nan while he fights and kills his
Archdemon
. Solas then transforms into his Dread Wolf form and goes to fight Lusacan. The blighted mage companion uses the tendrils to create a blighted construct called Elgar'nan's Wrath, which Rook's team defeats. Afterwards, Rook freed the blighted mage companion from her tendril pod, which brought her back to her senses. Lusacan attacks the roofs of their overhead building and blight tendrils begin erupting from the ground, which causes rubble to start falling on the group.
Morrigan
arrives to use her magic to protect them from the falling rubble and covers them as they make a run for the Divine's Manor.
Later on, the blighted mage companion revealed to Rook that the blight connected her to Elgar'nan which gave her insight into him. The blighted companion said that Elgar'nan's life force is tied to the
Veil
and his life is the only thing holding the Veil in place. In order to keep the Veil in place, Rook would need to tie the Veil to Solas as he would be the last surviving elven god. Rook also has the fake duplicate dagger of
Solas' lyrium dagger
which was made by the Veilguard when Rook was trapped in the
Fade prison
. Rook can use it in a bait and switch scheme to dupe Solas into using the fake dagger, causing a backlash, and rendering him helpless. Rook can also try to reason with Solas if Rook has the essence of a fragment of
Mythal
from the
Crossroads
. Coupled with Morrigan harboring Mythal's memories, Mythal's influence may be enough to persuade Solas to willingly tie himself to the Veil.
During
The Dread Wolf Rises
, Solas will tussle with Lusacan and Rook will fight Elgar'nan, but Solas is eventually ensnared by blight tendrils and Rook subdued with domination magic. While Elgar'nan is distracted, the blighted companion sneaks her way to the throne, using the blight tendrils as an interface, and commands the blight tendrils to release Solas. Her body slumps afterwards; depending on player choices, the blighted companion either dies from the exertion or becomes temporarily unconscious and breaks out of the tendrils after Elgar'nan is defeated and the world saved.
Quests
In Entropy's Grasp
Echoes of the Past
The Demon's Bargain
Hummingbird's Lament
Through the Shadows
The Forest of Spirits
Approval
Main article:
Bellara Lutare/Approval
To earn approval from Bellara, lean into curiosity, compassion, and respect for ancient knowledge and technology. She is interested in elvish history, unusual magic, and exploring the Fade, and she appreciates support of her scattered ideas rather than dismissal or impatience. She generally prefers cooperation and empathy toward others, though she disapproves when those who have been cruel toward the Dalish are not punished.
Disapproval comes from arrogance, cruelty (especially to elves), or abruptly shutting down discussion.
Romance
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
Rook first meets Bellara during
In Entropy's Grasp
. She is eager to help from the start and is very interested in Rook's goal of taking down the gods. Upon returning to the
Lighthouse
, Bellara will fix the eluvian, and shortly after, she will be found in her room struggling to fix the
Nadas Dirthalen
. Rook will have their first opportunity to flirt with her during this conversation, encouraging her that with her intelligence she can do what she sets her mind to. She admits that past relationships have struggled with her rambling, and Rook can flirt again, reassuring her that they could listen to her ramble all day.
Rook can next flirt with Bellara during
Echoes of the Past
, expressing delight that she has picked them for her team and assuring her they are eager to help; they can flirt again in the kitchen after
The Siege of Weisshaupt
and
Fire and Ice
.
Rook can express romantic interest in Bellara during a fiction-writing scene in her room at the Lighthouse, which becomes available after completing
Fire and Ice
and its immediate follow-up conversation with Bellara. Bellara is delighted, if nervous, to hear of Rook's romantic interest, and she explains that while she has some limited romantic experience with
Irelin
, she has no idea how a real relationship is supposed to go. However Rook reassures her, she agrees to explore things slowly with Rook.
Later, after meeting Cyrian in the woods during
Hummingbird's Lament
, Bellara will bring up their relationship at the Lighthouse and ask if Rook is sure they want to continue. At this point, Rook can choose to commit to an exclusive relationship with Bellara or break it off. Bellara will later be grateful for Rook's support at
Cyrian's funeral
. She will then have a follow-up conversation at the Lighthouse regarding her writing project where she acknowledges her relationship with Rook forms a central tenet of the work, and she reminisces about the day she met Rook.
If romanced, Rook will be able to speak to Bellara in her room after beginning
The Shadowed Sun
(but before beginning
Isle of the Gods
). She explains to Rook how much she cares about them and says they help her feel calm and anchored, which she needs. She is also grateful that Rook likes her for who she is, rather than wanting to change her into someone "better," and hopes they will be able to stay together after the final fight against the gods. As Rook leaves, she asks them to stay and pulls them into an embrace as the camera fades to black.
If Bellara is not chosen to disable the wards during
Isle of the Gods
, she will come to Rook's room during
The Dragon and the Dread Wolf
. She initially has no idea what to say, pacing and consulting her notes as she struggles to express her gladness at Rook's return, and hopes she did not show up as one of their regrets in the
Fade prison
. She admits she struggled to believe Rook would return safely and wants to live in this moment with Rook as long as she can. When Rook takes her hands, she states, "Mala lath athim, mir uthenera," which likely translates to something like, "Your love humbles me, my eternal dream."
Rook kisses her in response, and the camera fades down to her feet and away. Afterwards, Bellara tells Rook how much she loves them, and Rook confirms they love her too.
Rook will have an opportunity to speak to Bellara during
One Last Breath
, just prior to the final assault on
Elgar'nan
. If the romance options are taken, Bellara will be eager for a happy-ever-after and reaffirm to Rook how much she loves them.
If Bellara has been chosen to disable the wards during
Isle of the Gods
, and therefore is kidnapped during
The Dragon and the Dread Wolf
, she will come to Rook's room after the battle with Elgar'nan is over. While her eyes still have dark circles from the Blight, she appears otherwise normal, and she thanks Rook for saving her and apologizes for attacking them. She asks where that leaves them romantically, and Rook reassures her of their commitment.
Bellara explains that when they changed her, they told her no one was coming and fed the part of her heart that did not want to be happy. However, her faith in Rook was unwavering; she knew Rook would save her, even though she still struggles to believe it's all over and the Evanuris are finally dead. Regardless, she wants to live in this moment with Rook, and she recites the same "Mala lath athim, mir uthenera" line before the camera fades away.

Decoding Solas's  Eluvian
Notes
Bellara's last name was misspelled during early marketing as Lutara instead of Lutare. It was later corrected by her writer, John Epler.
Trivia
Bellara was written by
John Epler
.
Bellara was one of the first companions to be heard. In
Dragon Age 4 - Official Behind the Scenes Teaser Trailer
, actress Jee Young Han, portraying Bellara, is shown saying: "No, no, no, it's okay... That's the
good
kind of rumble".
Despite being a mage, Bellara wields a gauntlet that can turn into a hunting bow. The gauntlet was discovered by the Veil Jumpers and originally was created to make manipulating magic easier.
Bellara discovered that the gauntlet artifact made magical arrows and also resonated with other artifacts.
Bellara's
vallaslin
honors
Dirthamen
.
Bellara's favorite food is pan-fried fish.
Lutare is a family name rather than a clan name.
Bellara was originally intended to be 28-29, but has now evolved to be around 31-32.
BioWare polled the Dragon Age game developers with the question "
If our companions worked at the studio what would their jobs be?",
Bellara's top vote was ART with runner-up votes being in IT and programming. The included commentary stated:
“Bellara is very creative, so she’d work in the Art department.”
“Game Programmer, because she’s always tinkering and building things.”
“Definitely IT because Bellara is a mad genius."
Bellara likes to write stories as a hobby. She tells Taash it's easier to work on her writing with background noise, like someone else talking
.
Irelin
was in a relationship with Bellara. It ended some time before the start of
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. If pursuing a relationship with Rook, Bellara will reveal her relationship with Irelin was the only one she's had.

Tarot card

Appearances
Dragon Age II
Legacy
Mark of the Assassin
Heroes of Dragon Age
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
Bethany Hawke
(born 9:11
Dragon
) is
Hawke
's younger sister and
Carver
's fraternal twin. An
apostate
mage
, she received her magical training from her
father
, who was also an apostate.
Background
Bethany's magic manifested when she was nine, when she threw a local bully across a field without touching him. Her family was forced to move in all haste, eventually settling on the outskirts of
Lothering
.
While she was noted for her curiosity about religious stories,
she mostly stayed hidden away at home. This was, of course, because of her arcane talents. A traveler identified as only 'Sade' visited the Hawke family after the onset of Bethany's gifts, describing her as 'reveling in the stories of the far and away' and 'masterfully reserved'. Once, when she and a childhood friend of the Hawke siblings, Ebrin '
Peaches
' Janith, were being chased by a wild dog, Bethany accidentally made it go to sleep. After this incident, Bethany kept herself in even more secrecy. The friend did not betray her to the
templars
because 'she was too nice to be magic.'
Leliana
frequently interacted with Bethany as part of her duties and activities in the Lothering chantry.
Appearance
Bethany's appearance varies with Hawke's facial preset and skintone
Bethany has one of several facial structures and hair styles, depending on the customization of the player's character. The selection works as follows:
There are three base facial shapes that each family member can have. Hawkes using the default Hawke appearance or any of the first three presets will get the first head shape, Hawkes using the second three presets will get the second head shape, and Hawkes using the final three presets (or any modded presets past that) will get the third shape.
Each of the three head shapes has three variations - one with a pale skintone, one with a medium skintone, and one with a dark skintone, and each with a different hairstyle. Which of these variations is used by the game depends on Hawke's skintone; the first four skintones will result in the light-skinned version of Bethany, the second four in the medium-skinned version, and the final four in the dark-skinned version. All versions of Bethany will have black hair. The same applies for
Carver
,
Leandra
and
Gamlen
.
During
Varric
's initial recount of Hawke's flight from
Lothering
, Bethany appears with her default face/hair.
Involvement
Dragon Age II
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age II
.
As Lothering is overrun with Darkspawn, Bethany is forced to flee with her family. In a surprise encounter with an ogre, and if Hawke is a
mage
, Bethany will attack an
ogre
, be grappled, smashed to the ground and killed. However, if Hawke is a warrior or rogue, Carver takes her place. Following the fight, Bethany accompanies her family to
Kirkwall
.
Upon arrival, she spends a year working with her elder sibling in servitude to repay their passage into Kirkwall. Afterwards, Bethany and Hawke overhear about
The Deep Roads Expedition
and agree to join as a last resort to acquire enough wealth and influence to cow the templars from scrutinizing them too closely. Though she appreciates the trouble her family took to keep her out of the Circle, the running, hiding, and constant fear for what their future would hold have taken their toll. She begins to express guilt when she sees how much the family sacrifices to keep her free, especially when she
learned
their mother gave up her life as a noble to elope with their father. Sometimes, she even wonders whether everyone would be better off if she'd been sent to the Circle from the very start. Through their time in Kirkwall, Bethany and Hawke work on reclaiming the
family estate
. After amassing the fifty
sovereigns
needed to fund the expedition, Bethany volunteers to join her elder sibling in the
Deep Roads
venture. Just as they prepare to go,
Leandra
begs Hawke to leave Bethany at home, as the expedition is too dangerous. Though Bethany remarks that she is "allowed to fight the
darkspawn
," but the final decision is made by Hawke.
Left at home
If Bethany is left behind in Kirkwall when Hawke sets out for the expedition, she is arrested by
templars
lead by Knight-Captain
Cullen
and taken to the
Gallows
just as Hawke returns from the Deep Roads. She remains in contact with her family and adjusts quickly to life in the
Circle
. She passes her
Harrowing
and becomes a senior member of the Circle, in charge of teaching young apprentices. A
letter
reveals that
Ella
has taken quite a shine to her. She is relieved that she doesn't have to run anymore and finds life in the Circle bearable.
During the
Qunari
siege of Kirkwall
, she leads an attempt to save
First Enchanter
Orsino
from the Qunari. Hawke and their companions arrived later on and dispatched the Qunari attackers. After Orsino regained consciousness, Orsino used his magic to revive Bethany, who was the only other Circle mage to survive the Qunari attack. Hawke is coldly received by Bethany, who utters sarcastic and ungrateful remarks, but she survives the siege.
As hostilities between mages and templars increase, Bethany is
kidnapped
by
Grace
and
Thrask
who are inciting a rebellion to overthrow Meredith, believing that the Champion is working for the Knight-Commander, regardless of their actual allegiances in the mage-templar struggle.
During
The Last Straw (Quest)
, Bethany naturally wants to take the mages' side. If Hawke sides with the mages she's thankful to Hawke for siding with her kind and joins the party once again. She will then participate with the defense and evacuation of the Circle. If Hawke joins forces with the templars, she pleads with the Champion to not force her to choose between her family and her people. She can later be found in the courtyard of the Gallows, and asks Hawke if they'll fight her. If Hawke retorts by questioning if she would fight her own sibling as well, she responds by expressing doubt. However, Orsino convinces her that the mages must remain united during this crisis and she follows him into the Gallows.
The next time she is encountered is during the battle with Orsino. When he reveals that he studied the research of the
Blood Mage
Quentin
, Hawke may mention that he was the man that murdered their mother, causing Bethany to withdraw from Orsino, shocked and appalled that Orsino protected the man that killed their mother, and claiming the First Enchanter could have saved Leandra. As Orsino begins his blood magic ritual, Bethany expresses her dismay that Orsino is planning on killing Hawke, whom he had promised to spare. She joins the battle against his
Harvester
form. After Orsino is killed, she still mourns him. When Knight-Commander
Meredith
prepares to execute her, Bethany accuses her of driving Orsino to such extremes but accepts her sentence, stating that she knows what will happen.
Hawke can now choose to let Meredith go through with the execution, or speak up in Bethany's defense. If Hawke defends her, she expresses her gratitude with a hug and re-joins Hawke's party in time for the final battle with Meredith.
If Hawke sides with the mages, Bethany is relieved. She can join the party defending the mages in the Gallows. In her final dialogue she comes to the conclusion that her magic comes from the Maker and he wouldn't want mages to be treated as they are.
Taken on the Deep Roads
If taken on the Deep Roads expedition she contracts the
taint
and dies unless
Anders
is in the party, in which case she can be recruited into the Grey Wardens as the only recourse to save her life. If Bethany becomes a Grey Warden, she has very little contact with her family—contacting only Leandra to inform her of her survival and of her continued travels with the Wardens. She resents Hawke for coming out of the Deep Roads unscathed while she almost died and has been permanently changed. She's the only member of the Hawke family to return to Ferelden since their flight from Lothering.
During the Qunari siege in Act 2, she and several other Grey Wardens are attacked by the Qunari and receive support from Hawke. However, she cannot join Hawke to save the city from the Qunari. If Hawke tells her about the death of their mother, she tells Hawke that she already heard the news and that the Grey Wardens helped her hold a wake in her honor. She's glad Hawke was with her in her final moments.
Later, when tension between the templars and mages reaches its boiling point, she is abducted by Ser Thrask and used to get Hawke to join his cause. The plan fails however, and Thrask is killed along with the abductors.
Bethany can also be seen in the Deep Roads with
Nathaniel Howe
during the quest
Finding Nathaniel
, in which case she is surprised to see Hawke in the Deep Roads again. Bethany reveals that she is still sad and hurt. In addition to her fearing the day she will succumb to the taint, she is also grief-stricken by how much she misses her family. She explains that she tried to freeze herself emotionally to cope with her sorrow. Even so, she tries to accept that Hawke tried their best to save her. She does not give a proper goodbye.
During
The Last Straw (Quest)
Bethany arrives at the city in time to witness the start of the war. She finds Hawke and apologizes for her earlier attitude, thanks Hawke for saving her life, and wants to fight at Hawke's side. She no longer resents Hawke for taking her on the Deep Roads expedition. While she regrets that her life turned out this way, she takes solace in the Grey Wardens' noble purpose and doesn't regret helping them. Although she wishes she could have saved her brother Carver, she believes that whatever tribulations she has endured is all a part of the
Maker
's grand plan. When asked about how her role as a Warden could allow her to intervene when Grey Wardens are supposed to be neutral, her only reply is: "Right now, I'm not a Grey Warden. I'm your sister." She naturally wants to side with the mages, but still rejoins the party if Hawke sides with the templars.
Bethany Hawke Alive and Well:
In the
DLC
Legacy
, a deranged sect of the
Carta
tries to kidnap Bethany due to her link to "the blood of the
Hawke
". Bethany can travel with Hawke to a secret
Warden
prison
in the
Vimmark Mountains
in order to find the source of the aggression and uncover the harsh truth about the Hawke lineage.
Dragon Age: Inquisition
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
If Bethany is still alive, the
Inquisitor
can ask
Varric Tethras
about her fate.
Bethany became a Grey Warden:
Varric states that she was last seen on Warden business near the border of the
Anderfels
. A conversation with Hawke reveals that they sent their associate
Aveline Vallen
to take her far away from
Orlais
when they heard about
Grey Wardens
acting strangely. A bottle of her ritewine can be found in Redcliffe, titled "Princess Piss."
If Bethany was taken into the Deep Roads, Varric alludes to her fate during the
Keeping the Darkspawn Down
side quest if he is in the party.
Bethany became a Circle mage:
Varric states that she stayed behind in the Free Marches to help other mages from the Kirkwall Circle. A conversation with Hawke reveals that they sent their associate
Aveline Vallen
to take her far away from the
Free Marches
when they heard about
Grey Wardens
acting strangely.
Ultimately, if Hawke lives after the encounter with the
Nightmare
, it is mentioned that the Hawke siblings will likely reunite.

Friendship and Rivalry
See the
approval
page for more information.
Friendship with Bethany can generally be earned through acts of kindness, by siding with mages or by supporting family.
Rivalry with Bethany can generally be earned by choosing aggressive responses, by siding against mages or by exploiting Fereldans.

Trivia
Jennifer Hepler
wrote Bethany for
Dragon Age II
.
Bethany was named in honor of her maternal grandmother Bethann.
In the beginning of the game, when Varric is telling the legend of the Champion, Bethany has much larger breasts, a subtle reference to Varric's tendency to exaggerate.
Bethany is a second cousin of the human of the
Magi Origin
, regardless of whether or not they are the Warden, through her mother
Leandra Amell
.
She will reference this if asked why her parents protected her from the Circle so much. If the Warden was a Human Mage (imported save), she also states that one of the children became the Hero of Ferelden.
Bethany is excited about the possibility of
Leandra
reclaiming the Amell estate; however, she never gets the chance to actually live in it.
In her codex page, it is revealed Bethany "greatly respects"
Hawke
and has a "teasing rivalry" with
Carver
.  It can also be noted that she "envied the way [Hawke] embraced magic" (if Hawke is a mage), as Bethany wanted a normal life for herself. If Hawke brings him along, Carver further explains Bethany's resentment of being different in the
Legacy DLC
and also mentions that Bethany never felt she could live up to
Malcolm
's expectations, implying that Bethany and Carver were close enough to be emotional confidants.
It is implied in dialogue that Carver is the second child. By extension, this would make Bethany the youngest of the three Hawke siblings.
Bethany and
Carver
are the only companions who do not have their own unique specialization; instead they use the
Force Mage
and
Templar
specializations, respectively, from the beginning of Act 2. In Act 1, they remain un-specialized.
Bethany does not have the option to learn spells from the Primal skill tree.
When approaching the
Qunari
compound at the Kirkwall Docks, Bethany mentions that
one
of them killed her best friends back in Lothering, which is a reference to Sten from
Dragon Age: Origins
.
In the Chantry, Bethany may mention
Leliana
, saying she misses her telling stories in Lothering.
If Bethany is taken to the
Circle
,
Isabela
often writes her letters and sends her "wicked" books to read.
During the Deep Roads Expedition, Bethany's face morph may switch to another existing face preset when infected with the Taint. However, this new face bears the taint markings, similar to
Wesley's
after he was infected.
Bethany was planned to be included in the cancelled Exalted March DLC for Dragon Age II. She was planned to have a wedding ceremony in the DLC which would be triggered if the player opted for Hawke not to marry their current love interest.
If Gaider could write another
Dragon Age
comic, it would be dedicated to Bethany, starting with that scene in
Lothering
and ending as a fleeing Circle Mage. It would also involve adventuring with
Sebastian Vael
in
Starkhaven
, and maybe
Nathaniel Howe
.
