======================================================================
PAGE: Lucanis Dellamorte
CATEGORIES: Abominations, Characters, Companions, Dragon Age: Tevinter Nights characters, Dragon Age: The Veilguard characters, Dragon Age: The Veilguard companions
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Lucanis_Dellamorte
======================================================================

“Death is my calling.”
Character
Lucanis Dellamorte
Title
Master Assassin
The Demon of
Vyrantium
First Talon
(c. 9:52 Dragon)
Race
Human
Gender
Male
Class
Rogue
Specialization
Assassin
Affiliation
Antivan Crows
Veilguard
Location
The
Ossuary
The
Lighthouse
Family
Caterina Dellamorte
(grandmother)
Illario Dellamorte
(cousin)
Voice
Zach Mendez
Appearances
Dragon Age: Tevinter Nights
The Wake
(mentioned)
Dragon Age: Vows & Vengeance
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Lucanis Dellamorte
is a Master Assassin in the
Antivan Crows
and a
companion
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
Background
Lucanis is an expert assassin who specializes in executing powerful
mages
.
Lucanis killed so many
blood mages
and
Venatori
, that people began calling him the "Demon of
Vyrantium
."
He is also known to be the poised and pragmatic grandson of
Caterina Dellamorte
,
First Talon
of the Antivan Crows. When Caterina trained him as a child, she would frequently hit him with her cane when he made mistakes. He hated her for years for being cruel, though now thinks it was her way to make sure he survived life as a Crow.
Although Lucanis views the Crows as a family business, he isn't interested in succeeding his grandmother as First Talon and would prefer to just focus on his work rather than be the center of attention.
However, despite his preferences, it is said that he is Caterina’s favorite, and the one she intends to name as her heir.
Involvement
Dragon Age: Tevinter Nights
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Tevinter Nights
.
The plot follows BioWare's own canon
, meaning it may not follow some of the player's choices in the games.
The Wigmaker Job
Lucanis and his cousin
Illario
accept an anonymous contract on
Ambrose Forfex
,
Tevinter
's premiere wigmaker and a
Venatori
blood mage
. With the help of Athima, an elf sent by their mysterious patron, Lucanis and Illario reach Forfex’s party through the roofs and floating aqueduct of
Vyrantium
. At the wig show, whose dead-eyed models sport fantastical, transforming hair, Lucanis can feel that the
Veil
is thinner than it should be.
After Illario steals the keys, Lucanis heads for Forfex’s workshop, single-handedly killing its eight guards. Illario returns as the last guard falls. They are caught by one of Forfex's elven slaves, Effe. Noticing her pitiful state, Lucanis tells her to leave and forget their faces. She expresses approval when he tells her that they’re here to kill Forfex. Inside the workshop, Lucanis finds multiple rows of legless slaves chained to the ceiling, their skin spotted with crystalized sores. Forfex is further in, feeding his slaves
red lyrium
. Despite the risk, Lucanis decides to make Forfex pay instead of simply fulfilling his contract.
He realizes that Forfex is keeping demons at bay by strengthening the Veil with an
elven artifact
. When he destroys it, chaos erupts at the party, which Forfex goes to investigate. He doesn't sound the alarm, as Effe and the other slaves have hidden the guards' corpses. Lucanis tells Illario to get the slaves out of the mansion while he deals with Forfex.
Demons have possessed the models, as well as some of the drunkest and most frightened guests. The wigs have transformed, gaining a serrated edge and moving of their own volition. When
blood magic
isn't enough to stop Lucanis, Forfex starts ingesting mouthfuls of red-lyrium infused hair and turns into an arachnid-like
abomination
. Lucanis takes some injuries, but eventually defeats the abomination. Without Forfex as an anchor, the other abominations disintegrate. After Forfex's party, he finds his cousin at an inn, and they depart for home.
As approximately forty guests were killed in addition to Forfex, the Wigmaker Job is too big to be hushed up, and Lucanis is nicknamed 'the Demon'.
Magister
Zara Renata
, one of Forfex's Venatori associates, resolves to lay low and observe Lucanis to discover and exploit his weaknesses.
Dragon Age: Vows & Vengeance
Lucanis is hired to assassinate Lord Alessio of House Borgiani. Disguised as a servant during the wedding of Lillemar Borgiani, he poisons Lord Borgiani's drink, causing him to choke; at which he offered to escort him to the kitchen to kill him out of sight. After serving elixir of
embrium
to Alessio, who soon realized Lucanis was his poisoner, the Crow proceeded to kill him with his blades. However, an unexpected earthquake suddenly teleported both Lucanis and Alessio's body back to the wedding, where he was spotted in front of witnesses, forcing him to flee.
Determined to find a place to hide out, Lucanis chose the ruins of House Amato, where he inadvertently discovered its last heir,
Drayden Kiel
, had returned. Revealing himself after hearing Drayden vowing vengeance on House Borgiani for the murder of their parents, Lucanis claimed responsibility for Alessio's death. He identified Drayden by name, revealing he was associated with their father, who hired him for protection when the local anomalies began a few years ago, and spoke of Drayden often. In turn, Drayden identified Lucanis as an Antivan Crow from his attire and the fact that he approached them without making a sound.
However, the conversation was interrupted by the arrival of Lillemar and her guards, who had tracked Lucanis. Lucanis then worked with Drayden and her companion
Nadia Carcosa
to fight the Borgiani soldiers, the former using House Amato's secret passages to sneak up on them. But when they reunited with Nadia and Drayden's nursemaid Eliza in the attic, Nadia is captured by the Borgiani captain, forcing everyone to surrender.
The party is escorted outside to meet Lillemar as the captain falsely claimed Drayden ordered Alessio's assassination. Eliza then identified the captain as the one who scarred her during the Amatos' assassination, forcing him to admit that Lillemar's grandfather was plotting to overthrow the Antivan monarchy and ordered the Amatos killed before Drayden's father could expose him and falsely framed them as conspirators.
Before the captain could say anything else, Lucanis decided to break his client confidentiality policy to identify the captain as the one who hired him, claiming to recognize his voice. Initially trying to deny Lucanis' words, the captain soon broke, arrogantly boasting that he never supported Alessio as head of House Borgiani for trying to redeem the family for his father's deeds and, convinced that Lillemar would drive her house to the brink of collapse, was hoping to seize power, only for his plans to fall to pieces when Lucanis botched Alessio's assassination. Lillemar and her guard seemingly kill the captain before making amends with Drayden. However, the captain took advantage of their distraction to attempt to kill Lillemar only for Drayden to get stabbed instead. Lillemar then decapitated the captain, with Lucanis commenting on the messiness of the kill.
Lucanis then got to work on cauterizing Drayden's stab wound and determined that the captain's dagger was coated with Black Rot, slowly turning Drayden's blood into an oil-like substance. After telling Nadia that only a
necromancer
could save Drayden, Lucanis bade his allies farewell as Lillemar escorted the two companions to
Nevarra
.
Short Story: The Wake
See also:
Short Story: The Wake
At some point Lucanis is believed to have died. Members of the Antivan Crows, including
Viago
and
Teia
, set up a wake for him. His cousin Illario becomes deeply affected by his death and gets drunk while constantly ruminating on childhood memories of him and Lucanis.
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
Around 9:51 Dragon, Lucanis' ship was ambushed by the
Venatori
on the way to kill a
magister
named
Calivan
, and his body was sent back to the Antivan Crows. However,
Caterina Dellamorte
, the First Talon and Lucanis's grandmother, suspected his death was faked and that the body bearing his face was altered by blood magic as a decoy. Furthermore, she also suspected a traitor within the Crows who helped attack Lucanis' ship; however, Caterina kept her suspicions to herself.
In actuality, Lucanis was taken to an underwater prison called the
Ossuary
, where
Magister
Zara Renata
of the
Venatori
forcefully inserted a
spirit-turned-"demon" of Spite
into Lucanis as part of her experiments in unwilling
possession
. Spite was given to Lucanis via something he ate, like "a parasite in uncooked meat,"
likely via the same method used to corrupt the
Seekers
during
Dragon Age: Inquisition
. This resulted in Lucanis becoming a rare non-
mage
abomination
; Zara was also entertained at the idea of turning his nickname of "Demon of
Vyrantium
" literal.
However, Spite was just as unwilling in the possession as Lucanis himself, developing a profound grudge against the Venatori and allowing his host to remain in control when awake. Out of their shared resentment towards their captors and fury at their mistreatment, they agreed to work together to escape, though the relationship remained strained due to its violent inception. Lucanis in particular developed severe insomnia in an attempt to give Spite as little control over their shared body as possible.
Lucanis remained trapped in the Ossuary for a full year, though it is unclear at what point Spite was introduced into his body. Zara continued to torture Lucanis in an attempt to force Spite to take over his body and change him into something more useful for her needs. She also created a
phylactery
from his blood and used it to control him, preventing him from harming either her or Calivan.
During
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
, Rook's disruption of Venatori plans
at the beginning of the game
allow Caterina to finally discover the presence of the Ossuary near Treviso, and when Rook arrives to request the assistance of the rumored Demon of Vyrantium, she asks Rook to rescue Lucanis from his prison. When the Veilguard arrives at the Ossuary, however, they discover Lucanis has recently broken containment and gone on a rampage in an effort to escape. The Venatori have narrowly managed to seal him in ice, only for him to break out (with Spite's help) at the moment of Rook's arrival and kill all present.
Upon being told the Veilguard require the talents of a magekiller against the
Evanuris
, Lucanis requests
Rook
help him destroy his phylactery and assist in killing the prison warden,
Calivan
, as per his original contract from a year ago. He also reveals that as an abomination, Lucanis can use Spite's magical abilities to make himself stronger. For example, Lucanis can summon magical wings that allow him to make superhuman leaps in battle, and Spite can be asked to use
Spite's Reach
as his in-game skill, which uses the instability of the
Veil
to summon floating platforms from the
Fade
to help the party cross gaps.
Once Calivan is dead, Lucanis and the Veilguard return to
Treviso
, only to discover Zara Renata has attacked the Cantori Diamond.
Teia Cantori
,
Viago de Riva
, and
Illario Dellamorte
express shock at Lucanis's return, but reveal that in the confusion of the attack,
Caterina Dellamorte
was tragically killed. While the circumstances of the attack are suspicious, Lucanis feels obligated to fulfill his contract with the Veilguard before he can fully dedicate his time to help the Crows.
Lucanis resides in the
Lighthouse
's pantry. Cooking is one of Lucanis' favored hobbies, and he is so proficient at it that he eventually becomes the Veilguard's predominant cook. He is also a coffee aficionado and uses it to keep himself awake.
Lace Harding
however, is concerned that using caffeine to deal with Spite isn't a long-term, healthy solution and could even kill him.
Later, Illario sets up a meeting with Lucanis and Rook to discuss recent intelligence regarding Zara. They meet up at Café Pietra, and Illario reveals that the Crows he sent after Zara say she's gone to
Vyrantium
. Lucanis doubts this intel and thinks there are traitors in the Crows giving Illario a false lead. He is confident that Zara is in Treviso and has Antivan Crow traitors protecting her. Lucanis insists that Illario investigate in Treviso, which upsets Illario, but he begrudgingly agrees to investigate the Crows for corruption before storming off. Lucanis comments that it's typical for Illario to cave under pressure and that he is also disinclined to listen to other people. As Lucanis enjoys his coffee, he dwells on how even before he was captured, his life has never been his own and so much has been determined for him. He ponders if he can ever live a full life.
[5]
After returning from recruiting
Davrin
, Rook receives word that
Ghilan'nain
has set loose two
blighted
dragons
, each attacking
Minrathous
and
Treviso
simultaneously. Both
Neve
and Lucanis urge Rook to help defend their home cities, and each leaves immediately to see to their defenses. Rook must choose to defend a city and leave the other to suffer its blighted dragon's rampage and the enemies taking advantage of it.
If Rook chooses to save Minrathous:
Lucanis is dispirited by the devastation of his city and leaves the team for a while to tend to his home. If Rook is in a romantic relationship with Lucanis, he will break up with them and says he cannot pursue a relationship in his current state. Lucanis will return after Rook completes recruiting all members of the Veilguard, eager to avenge his city.
If Rook chooses to save Treviso:
Lucanis helps Rook fight and chase away Corius the Icetalon, preventing further devastation to Treviso's canals and waterways by Blight.
Sometime later at the Lighthouse, Rook, Lace Harding, and
Taash
catch Spite in control of Lucanis' sleeping body, attempting to leave with him through the
eluvian
. Fortunately, Lucanis eventually wakes up and regains control. Rook urges Lucanis to find a better solution to his problem with Spite, but Lucanis assures Rook he has it under control and another incident will not occur.
Lucanis is tasked with assassinating Ghilan'nain during Ghilan'nain's
siege of Weisshaupt
. Rook's team infiltrates Weisshaupt through an
eluvian
the Grey Wardens had in storage and fight through
darkspawn
to make their way to
First Warden
Jowin
. After overcoming Jowin's obstinacy to Rook's guidance, Rook makes a plan to subdue Ghilan'nain's
archdemon
,
Razikale
, with an old dragon trap on the other side of
Weisshaupt
.
Rook uses the
lyrium dagger
that Ghilan'nain desires as bait and lures Razikale to the dragon trap and subdues it. The First Warden insists on landing the killing blow to the Archdemon, but Ghilan'nain appears in physical form and turns him into a
blood sacrifice
in order to transform Razikale into a multi-headed monster. Despite Ghilan'nain using
blood magic
to enhance her archdemon, the Veilguard defeats Razikale, and
Davrin
strikes the killing blow to Razikale; however, he does not perish from slaying the Archdemon.
Instead, Razikale's magical energy is returned to Ghilan'nain, who shrieks angrily. Sensing that Ghilan'nain is vulnerable, Rook throws
Solas' lyrium dagger
to Lucanis, who magically lunges at Ghilan'nain. Unfortunately, she is able to narrowly escape, and Lucanis' assassination strike is only able to cut Ghilan'nain's face. Lucanis asks for another assassination attempt at Ghilan'nain, but Ghilan'nain has used the
blight
to overwhelm Weisshaupt's defenders, ultimately forcing Rook to order the Veilguard and any survivors they can rescue to retreat back to the eluvian.
Back at the Lighthouse, the Grey Wardens' heavy casualties dispirit Davrin and the team. The Veilguard speak of the pros and cons of the battle, but ultimately, Davrin considers it a failure since they failed to kill Ghilan'nain. Lucanis confronts Davrin, saying he is really insinuating that Lucanis's failure to kill Ghilan'nain is the source of the defeat. While Harding tries to assuage Lucanis' guilt, Davrin interjects to agree; he
does
hold Lucanis responsible and accuses
Spite
of sabotaging the mission.
Lucanis retorts that Davrin, as a Warden, has
blight
in his veins and could have been influenced or controlled by Ghilan'nain. Before the Veilguard can descend to infighting, Rook orders the cessation of further arguments and refocuses the team on their mission.
Emmrich
states that Spite is a creature of raw emotion; it is possible Lucanis' issues with Spite may have distracted him.
Harding
adds that every member of the team has been distracted by their own issues, and they may need to deal with them if they are to fight the
war
against the
elven gods
at their very best.
Although a Veilguard companion and Davrin think that what happened to Weisshaupt and the devastated city of the
blighted
dragon
attack are more than just mere distractions, Rook thinks that whatever they do next, they'll need to face their problems with clear heads. Should Rook speak to Lucanis after the fall of Weisshaupt, he expresses disappointment at his failure and notes he has never failed to fulfill a contract before. He had assumed he was just as efficient as before, but it is clear to him now that he was distracted and he needs to get his head on straight. Lucanis encourages Rook to help resolve the other companions' issues as well—especially Davrin. Eventually, Lucanis and Davrin let bygones be bygones after getting drunk together and exchanging amusing tales of their past escapades.
Eventually, Lucanis receives word from Viago that Venatori have been sighted in Treviso, beginning the quest
Bloodbath
. If Rook chose to save Minrathous, Lucanis additionally reveals that these Venatori have been seen among the relief effort in Treviso, and Lucanis suspects them to be Zara's minions. While Illario has continued to assert Zara is in Vyrantium and these Venatori are merely a distraction, Lucanis has secretly paid an obscene amount of money to a desperate informant, who has revealed Zara is hiding in the Treviso
Chantry
. Lucanis has assumed he must deal with Treviso's devastation alone, but he is determined to kill Zara and requests Rook's help.
Rook, Lucanis, and their companion navigate through Treviso to the Chantry, only to discover Illario waiting on the nearby rooftops. He tries to coax Lucanis into letting him participate in Zara's undoing, but Lucanis states Illario isn't suitable for the mission and insists he stay out of the job for his own safety. Illario reluctantly relents and yields to Lucanis' request.
Rook and Lucanis fight Zara's minions in the Treviso Chantry and eventually find Zara bathing in a pool of blood. The Veilguard defeat Zara in battle, revealing her true form as an elderly crone rather than a beautiful young woman, and in the aftermath, Zara tries to negotiate for her life by leveraging the identity of who betrayed Lucanis. While Lucanis desires the truth, Spite wants to give her an immediate, excruciating death. Before she can reveal the truth, however, Illario suddenly drops from the rafters and kills Zara by crushing her throat.
Having his kill stolen from him infuriates Spite, who takes control of Lucanis and attempts to stab Illario to death. Lucanis, battling for control over his own body, struggles to stop Spite and begs Rook to get Illario away from him. Before Rook can intervene, Illario pinches his brooch—a gold bird with spread wings—and uses blood magic to subdue both Lucanis and Spite.
In the aftermath, Illario feigns ignorance of his control over Lucanis and demands Rook keep Lucanis away from the Crows and Treviso, as he has become a danger to his family. Back at the Lighthouse, Lucanis apologizes to Rook for losing control of Spite and resolves to find a solution to the problem. Rook can then express their suspicion that there is something strange and possibly untrustworthy about Illario.
Later,
Taash
and Rook again catch Spite in control of Lucanis in the Lighthouse pantry. Taash has managed to contain them both for the moment, and Rook sends them away to secure the eluvian while Rook speaks to Spite. Spite reveals that Lucanis hasn't been upholding the deal they made long ago: "Break [their] chains. Kill. Escape [their] prison. And live."
Rook asks for clarification since Lucanis has clearly escaped the Ossuary, but Spite insists that isn't true and he wants out. Spite becomes visibly upset that Lucanis isn't fulfilling his promise, and he urges Rook to force him to uphold his end of the bargain. However, Spite is interrupted when Lucanis wakes up and regains control of his body. Once again, Lucanis apologizes to Rook for losing control, but Rook encourages Lucanis to seek help from his companions. At this point, Rook can begin a romantic relationship with Lucanis by assuring him they are not intimidated by his current struggles.
Later, at the Lighthouse, Lucanis enlists
Emmrich Volkarin
's help to get the truth out of Zara Renata's corpse. Emmrich uses his
corpse whispering
on Zara, and her possessed corpse reveals that she and Illario were secretly lovers before he betrayed her. Zara says Illario was jealous that Lucanis was being groomed to become
First Talon
, a title that Illario coveted more than anything. Illario conspired with Zara to kill Lucanis, but Zara went against Illario's wishes and had Lucanis imprisoned and tortured, since she thought Lucanis would make a promising experimental subject. Furthermore,
Elgar'nan
gifted Illario his bird-like brooch which allows him to use blood magic to control Spite. Having heard enough, Lucanis has Emmrich end the corpse-whispering session and plots to punish Illario by taking everything from him.
If Rook chooses to save Treviso:
Lucanis eventually reveals he has had word from Viago and Teia regarding information about Illario. However, it is too dangerous to meet in Treviso, where they know there are spies, and they have requested a secret meeting in Minrathous instead. Rook and Lucanis meet with the Talons at the Cobbled Swan, which starts the quest
Inner Demons
.
Viago reveals that one of his spies has found Caterina's opal ring in the trash of Villa Dellamorte, implying Illario is keeping Caterina imprisoned there. Caterina's opal ring was a gift to Lucanis' mother and was a mark of her favor; neither she nor Illario would ever part with it lightly. (Lucanis suggests that if Illario knew it was missing, he would be tearing Treviso apart to find it.) Therefore, it is most likely a signal from Catarina herself that she is alive and trapped within the villa. Teia wants to rescue Caterina immediately, while Viago thinks it could be misinformation to lure them into a trap and proposes they just attack Illario.
Lucanis is unsure of the right course of action and fears he might get Caterina killed if he makes a wrong move. Spite, infuriated by the indecision, takes over Lucanis and asks Rook to help them. When Rook agrees, Spite transports both of them to a portion of the
Fade
made to look like the Ossuary. Spite says Lucanis has locked himself away in this mental Ossuary, and he can't reach him; worse, since Lucanis and Spite are joined, if he's trapped there, then so is Spite, which frustrates the spirit.
Rook and Spite make their way to Lucanis' cell, meeting various, imagined loved ones of Lucanis that reflect the negative thoughts he has about himself. They meet Caterina, who reflects Lucanis' fear of being rejected by her; Harding, who reflects his fear that he is a danger to his comrades; Neve, who reflects his hopelessness in his ability to overcome his problems; and Illario, who reflects Lucanis' defeatism and fear of not only failing, but hurting his friends and loved ones in the process. Rook offers Lucanis advice and encouragement thoughout, helping Lucanis vanish these symbolic guards.
Rook and Spite eventually make their way to Lucanis, and Rook deduces that Lucanis hides himself away in this mental Ossuary because he is afraid to face the challenges of his new life, including learning to live as an abomination. Ultimately, Lucanis is afraid that if he fails, he'll hurt the ones he loves. Lucanis frustratingly comes to the conclusion that Rook is right but doesn't know how to begin in making progress. Rook suggests that Lucanis and Spite start by making a goal they both agree on and make it happen. Rook can then encourage them to endeavor to punish Illario or save Caterina.
Lucanis and Spite agree to these terms, and their bond becomes more harmonious after forming this new deal. Rook and Lucanis return to the Cobbled Swan with Teia, who asks if they're all right and reveals that they fell into a stupor for a few seconds. Lucanis reassures the Crow Talons he and Rook are fine, then orders Viago to keep an eye on Illario while he plans Caterina's rescue. Later, he will receive word from Viago that Illario plans to host a soiree at the villa for all the Talons, and Lucanis decides this is the time to move against him.
At this point, Lucanis will cook a large meal for the Veilguard. If Rook is romancing Lucanis, this meal will include a dish which pairs well with their favorite drink; if Rook is not romancing Lucanis or Neve, he will have made Neve a gooseberry pie, and Rook may tease him about his burgeoning romance with her.
If Rook chooses to save Minrathous:
Some time later, Lucanis tells Rook he has received a note from Viago confirming that Caterina has been alive this whole time; Illario faked her death and has been imprisoning her in Villa Dellamorte ever since. Furthermore, Illario is making a bid for First Talon and is hosting the other Crow Talons at Villa Dellamorte at an upcoming soiree. Lucanis thinks he will use his magical brooch to control them with blood magic, or use bribery or hostages to coerce them to follow him. Rook and Lucanis set off to thwart Illario.
During
A Murder of Crows
, Lucanis and the Veilguard infiltrate the Dellamorte Estates to set Caterina free. After defeating the Venatori guards, Lucanis has a tender reunion with Caterina. Rook suggests they escort Caterina to safety while they and Lucanis deal with Illario at the Dellamorte opera house, but Caterina refuses to be rescued and insists she will join them there on her own.
At the Dellamorte opera house, Illario is trying to persuade the Antivan Crows that Caterina's death and Lucanis' status as a dangerous abomination have forced Illario to take on the mantle of First Talon. Furthermore, he believes his alliance with the Venatori will be a boon to their reclaiming of
Antiva
from the
Antaam
. However, Lucanis (with Rook alongside) suddenly breaks into the opera house, declaring Illario will become First Talon over his dead body and shocking the gathered Crows.
Illario accepts Lucanis' challenge and uses his bird-like brooch to inflict blood magic on Lucanis, but since Lucanis and Spite are in harmony, they are able to successfully resist his blood magic. With the Veilguard's support, Lucanis defeats Illario and his Venatori. While Illario tells Lucanis to kill him, Lucanis thinks his humiliating defeat in front of every Crow house will satiate his vengeance more. Caterina reveals herself and approaches Illario, telling him to accept his defeat with dignity, as no one from House Dellamorte kneels. Lucanis will then decide Illario's fate.
If Treviso was blighted or if Rook advises him to do so, Lucanis will punish Illario with imprisonment and orders Viago to take him away. If Treviso isn't blighted, Rook can cite that Illario is still special to Lucanis as he is one of his few friends and family members and can suggest he should forgive him. In this case, Lucanis has Viago take Illario away and asks him to keep an eye on him.
Regardless, Illario protests being sentenced by Lucanis, as only the First Talon has that authority, but Caterina validates Lucanis's judgment by formally declaring him First Talon. Lucanis confides to Rook that despite his new title, Caterina will continue issuing the orders to the Crows on his behalf until he finishes his contract with the Veilguard. In defiance of having lost so much, Lucanis resolves to fight for the people who matter in his life.
If
Rook
saved Minrathous and completed the Antivan Crow quests:
After the Veilguard secures the succession of the Crows, Lucanis returns to the Lighthouse with Rook and reveals he has resolved to separate himself from Spite once his contract with Rook is fulfilled—even if the endeavor is dangerous. Spite yearns for freedom from their connection as well, but most "curing" from possession involves destroying the demon in the Fade, which Lucanis wants to avoid.
Despite everything, Lucanis feels obligated to help Spite separate from him peacefully and even feels he'll miss him. He is also concerned about how their bond has molded each other; he admits he has grown stronger from their bond, and Spite has obtained his form and skills. Lucanis also worries without their connection, Lucanis may unleash a true "Demon of Vyrantium" to terrorize the world. Even so, Rook tells Lucanis he must face the consequences of obtaining independent lives for him and Spite, which inspires Lucanis to see through his endeavor.
If Rook completed
Inner Demons
and completed the Antivan Crow quests:
During
A Moment's Peace
, Lucanis and Rook celebrate Lucanis' and Spite's newfound alliance by having drinks at a café owned by the Crows. Both Lucanis and Spite intend to stay together and strengthen their bond. Lucanis has even reserved a spare seat and cup of coffee for Spite, though Spite is perplexed by the coffee, noting it smells like Lucanis.
Lucanis tells Rook that while the other Talons are wary of having an abomination as First Talon, they at least prefer him to his cousin, Illario, and the Venatori. Caterina has maintained her composure and distracts herself with work, despite Lucanis thinking she is secretly still upset by him being an abomination and Illario's betrayal. Lucanis is content with his new life, even if it isn't normal.
During
Isle of the Gods
, Lucanis aids the Veilguard as they assault
Tearstone Island
in an effort to assassinate Ghilan'nain and prevent the elven gods from crafting a red lyrium dagger. In a final confrontation with Ghilan'nain, either
Davrin
or
Lace Harding
sacrifice his/her life to distract Ghilan'nain so that Lucanis can successfully land a killing blow to Ghilan'nain. The ruse works, and Lucanis is able to kill Ghilan'nain with Solas' lyrium dagger, though he appears to die as Rook is pulled into the Fade by Solas.
However, this image is only a ruse, and Lucanis survives to participate in the
Battle of Minrathous
. Lucanis can die during
The Last Gambit
or during
The Dread Wolf Rises
depending on player choices.
Quests
Sea of Blood
Coffee with the Crows
(outing)
Bidding Farewell
(conversation)
Bloodbath
(quest) (given as a main quest if Minrathous was saved)
Inner Demons
(outing) (if Treviso was saved)
A Murder of Crows
(quest)
A Moment's Peace
(conversation) (if Treviso was saved)
Fit for a Crow
(gift)
Approval
Main article:
Lucanis Dellamorte/Approval
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
Lucanis's approval can be gained by supporting the Crows, by taking direct action whenever possible, and by being willing to accept a certain amount of risk. He has little patience for indecision or moral grandstanding that ignores obvious consequences. He favors the quick pragmatic solution over lengthy diplomacy, especially when the goal can be achieved quickly.
Lucanis also appreciates support in his situation with Spite. Flippancy or dismissal of his concerns, or showing fear and distrust of him as an abomination, will result in disapproval.
If Treviso is blighted, Lucanis's approval will slow dramatically.
Romance
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
Lucanis first meets Rook at the Ossuary during
Sea of Blood
. He is initially wary of Rook, but the two agree to help one another with their end goals.
Upon returning to the
Lighthouse
, Rook, Neve, and Bellara learn about Lucanis' strenuous relationship with his demon, Spite. After Spite lashes out at Lucanis, Rook is able to express romantic interest for the first time by voicing concern that Lucanis not be left alone with the demon.
Lucanis later invites Rook to Treviso to meet with his cousin, Illario, at Cafe Pietra. Along the way Lucanis and Rook can shop for items for other members of the Veilguard team. Rook can prompt Lucanis to consider himself as well; to which Rook buys him a wyvern-tooth dagger. The purchase prompts Lucanis to reveal he has loved wyverns since he was a young boy. At the cafe, Lucanis makes a note of which drink Rook prefers between coffee, tea, or hot chocolate.
If Treviso is not saved during
On Deadly Wings
, Lucanis will refuse to romance Rook. If Treviso is saved, Lucanis will be grateful and will remain romanceable.
After
Bloodbath
, Spite will attempt to walk Lucanis out of the pantry in an effort to get free of an unknown prison. After talking him down, Rook will be able to express interest in a romantic relationship with Lucanis by stating they are not afraid of walking the edge of a bad idea. He will nearly kiss Rook, but will step away at the last moment and move outside to clear his head.
He will continue to remain reticent due to his concerns over Spite and his uncertainty of himself. The quest
Inner Demons
will provide a chance for Lucanis and Spite to unify their differences; it turns out Lucanis has been, either intentionally or not, keeping his mind trapped in the Ossuary, as its already-escaped prison seemed safer than facing his new reality as an abomination. Rook will be able to express romantic concern over his well-being, and Lucanis will despairingly suggest Rook leave rather than be hurt by him.
Rook will persuade Lucanis to leave the prison by helping him work together with Spite to either punish Illario or rescue Caterina. While he is worried about the difficulty of beginning again, Rook assures him they will be there to help. They will also be able to flirt with him again in front of Teia and Viago after emerging from the dream.
The next time Rook goes to the Lighthouse, Lucanis will have prepared a large meal for the team. He will have also made Rook their favourite drink with an accompanying dessert of either
Nevarran hazelnut torte
, tarta di limon, or churros. At this point, Rook can lock in the Lucanis romance, which will break off all other romance paths.
After
A Murder of Crows
, the wrap-up scene at the Lighthouse will feature little romance content save an acknowledgement of Rook being important to Lucanis; he is worried about Illario and suspects the Crows will demand him for First Talon responsibilities soon. However, this will be followed by a second quest,
A Moment's Peace
, which takes place after
For the Love of Treviso
, where Rook will accompany Lucanis and Spite to Cafe Pietra for coffee. There, Lucanis will enjoy a moment's peace with Spite and Rook and begins to wonder what his new life might look like -- with Rook.
Once Rook returns from the Fade in
A Cage for Gods
, Lucanis is both overjoyed and in disbelief that the team managed to find Rook. He meets Rook in their room to reaffirm their survival -- for both of them -- and spends the night with Rook, with Spite's wings providing an erstwhile blanket.
Lucanis will also have romance-specific dialogue in the final stages of
One Last Breath
. He expresses his gratitude for Rook and apologizes that he has not been clear with his feelings; while he was afraid of many things before, including Spite and his own heart, he can now tell Rook clearly to their face that he loves them. If they can kill the gods here, he only wants to fall asleep in their arms.

Trivia
Lucanis was written by
Mary Kirby
and Courtney Woods.
When something taps into
the Fade
, Lucanis feels an itch in the back of his eyeballs.
Lucanis likes coffee,
and is a good cook.
He is in his mid-thirties.
Banter between
Lucanis
and
Emmrich
indicates that Lucanis once attempted to woo
Viago
with a gift of knives. He was unsuccessful.
According to creative director John Epler, Spite momentarily leaves Lucanis during intimate times out of respect for his privacy.
Lucanis has a fascination with wyverns.
Normally, only Lucanis can see
Spite
, who he can talk to as a separate person as they share a body.
If
Rook
doesn't start a romance with Lucanis or Neve, then Lucanis starts a romance with
Neve Gallus
.

“I am Lukesh. Fear me!”
Character
Lukesh
Title
Advisor
Race
Human
Gender
Male
Class
Mage
Appearances
Dragon Age Legends
Lukesh
is the advisor to the Viscount of
Kaiten
in
Dragon Age Legends
.
Background
Lukesh was part of the band of heroes that aided
Ravi
in preventing an
abomination
, Ravi's uncle
Khedra
, from destroying the
Free Marches
city of Kaiten. He is later entrusted with caring for the Viscount's son,
Eiton
.
Involvement
Years later he encounters the
Legends Hero
and joins them as a
companion
after both he and Eiton are rescued from
demons
under the command of a witch named
Soliel
.
Eventually it is revealed that the
Pride demon
that Ravi's band of heroes vanquished long ago survived by implanting the shards of its soul in several vessels. These vessels amassed disparate followers comprising of
Disciple
darkspawn
, demons, and cultists—all of them forming a coalition to get revenge on Ravi and his allies. Eventually Lukesh helped Ravi and the Legends Hero defend Kaiten from the Pride demon's followers.
Starting abilities
Concept art
Mana Drain
Frostbite
Lightning
Storm
Mass Heal
Magic Shield
Note:
For a level 15 character
Starting statistics
Attack: 28
Defense: 13
Stamina: 13
Agility: 24
Luck: 10
Note:
For a level 15 character

“Another grand scheme come to nothing.”
Character
Marquis of Serault
Title
Marquis
Race
Human
Gender
Male or Female
Location
Serault
Quests
Inquisition
:
Investigate Serault
Quell Unrest in Serault
Search for the Lost in Serault
Family
The Shame of Serault
(great-grandfather)
Marquis of Serault
(mother)
The Anchoress
(great half-aunt)
Unnamed Sibling
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Last Court
Dragon Age: Inquisition
(mentioned)
The
Marquis of Serault
is the
player-controlled
protagonist of
Dragon Age: The Last Court
. The player chooses either The Huntress (female) or The Scholar (male), each with different skills and abilities. The Marquis must navigate an important visit to the region from a notable person as well as maintain the safety, wealth and respect of the region during numerous difficulties and encounters during that time. The Marquis is in their thirties.
Background
“Payment in Glass”
― Traditional Family Motto of the House of Glass
The Marquis is the young scion of the
de Serault
family, nicknamed the
House of Glass
, and, having succeeded their mother, is the present ruler of the Marquisate of
Serault
. Serault is a domain located in the far west of
Orlais
. It is a realm of deep forests, stubborn people, and customs that border on heresy. The citizens of the region are largely craftsmen and the pride of the marquisate is its famed glasswork.
The Marquis' great grandfather, also a former
Marquis of Serault
, was an
apostate
mage
, who later became an
abomination
. He is described as brilliant, murderous, generous, romantic, and ruthless, but eventually he brought shame to the marquisate. His name is now forgotten; he is known now only as the
Shame of Serault
. The family was even stripped of their ancestral mask, the symbol of nobility in Orlais, by one of the
Divines
. It has been held in trust by the
Chantry
for over a century.
This history continues to haunt the region and the Shame's family.
Now the current Marquis of Serault sets out to regain Serault's lost stature, by whatever means required.
Initial Statistics
The Huntress
“Raised to rule. Trained in bow, sword and steed. Fearless.”
The Huntress is valiant, charismatic, and skilled in the hunt. She cares little for books or schemes.
Rulership
Scholarship
Derring-Do
Woods-wise
Cunning
30
20

30

The Scholar
“You divide your time between the great hall and the library's hush. This is your chance to free Serault from the shackles of tradition. Seize it.”
The Scholar is a little better at rulership. He is also learned, cunning, and has spent thirty years avoiding fencing lessons and hunting.

Note:
While the Scholar starts with 10 extra statistic points in total, the Huntress has 30 extra Derring-Do, the hardest stat to raise.
Involvement
Dragon Age: The Last Court
The Marquis of
Serault
receives news that
Divine Justinia V
is traveling to Serault on her way to the
Temple of Sacred Ashes
. There she will assess whether or not Serault has recovered from the humiliation that the
Shame of Serault
wrought. The Marquis of Serault sets out to use this opportunity to restore Serault to its former station at any cost.
Dragon Age: Inquisition
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The Marquis de Serault is involved in several
war table
operations with the
Inquisition
.
While investigating the death of
Divine
Justinia V, the Inquisition dispatches several agents to Serault to ascertain if the Divine's visit there was in anyway involved in her demise.
If Josephine sends an emissary:
The Inquisition's emissary is warmly welcomed by the Marquis. Later the Marquis and the Emissary mysteriously vanish while hunting the Deep Woods.
If Leliana sends scouts:
The Marquis of Serault is found by several inquisition scouts in a hut out in the deep woods, playing chess with the Inquisition emissary. The scouts report that the emissary's eyes are apparently now somewhat 'inhuman'. The emissary flees and evades capture. The Marquis meanwhile returns to Serault and grants the Inquisition some Serault Stained Glass as a reward for their aid.
If Cullen sends troops:
The emissary is found dazed and confused but the Marquis of Serault remains missing.
If Leliana sends spies to Serault initially:
Several Inquisition spies situate themselves in Serault to observe the region. Eventually in the wake of the chaos which sweeps over
Thedas
, the panic devolves into rioting in Serault.
If Cullen sends troops to quell the rioting:
The presence of Inquisition soldiers in Serault helps calm residents and help them feel safe, bringing a peacful resolution to the situation. The Marquis of Serault thanks the Inquisition with a reward of Serault Stained Glass.

There is a rumor that
the Cheery Baron
and the prior Marquis (this one's mother) were lovers, which can be found out during
an event
where a peasant jests about the parentage of the current Marquis.

======================================================================
PAGE: Marsa
CATEGORIES: Characters, Companions
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Marsa
======================================================================

Character
Marsa
Race
Human
Gender
Female
Class
Warrior
Location
Kirkwall
(formerly)
Appearances
Dragon Age Legends
Marsa
is a
warrior
and
companion
of the
Legends Hero
.
Background
Once a member of the
Kirkwall City Guard
, Marsa was either kicked out of the guard, or willingly left. If you ask her, she'll tell you the guard was becoming increasingly corrupt. Ask the guards of
Kirkwall
and they'll say she became violent and unstable.
Involvement
Marsa becomes a companion of the Legends Hero at some point during the Hero's journey.
While investigating
Lukesh
's disappearance in
Kirkwall
, the Legends Hero is forced to bargain with either the
Coterie
or the
Kirkwall City Guard
in order to get leads regarding Lukesh's whereabouts. However, the Coterie demands the Hero's party take out some city guards not under their employ before they help the Hero. Alternatively, the Hero can elect to cull the Coterie's numbers and interrogate whoever is left to obtain the information they require.
If the Legends Hero decides to seek the help of the Kirkwall City Guard, Marsa will say that the Kirkwall guard is vile and corrupt; she refuses to work with them again. Marsa will leave the party and won't rejoin until their buisness in Kirkwall is over. Marsa can later be found outside the city, defending herself from Kirkwall guards seeking to kill her. The guards are eventually attacked by
darkspawn
from the the trees and the Legends Hero can let the guards die, which will please Marsa, or help the guards fight off the darkspawn, which will cause Marsa to be vexed that they helped them. If the guards are helped, then they quickly run off after the battle in order to not have to thank the Legends Hero or Marsa for their assistance.
If the Legends Hero elects to wage war on the Coterie, Marsa will run into a Kirkwall guard named Kedra, who she sees as a traitor and whom she has a hostile relationship with.
References

======================================================================
PAGE: Martine
CATEGORIES: Characters, Companions, Grey Wardens
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Martine
======================================================================

Character
Martine
Race
Human
Gender
Female
Class
Warrior
Specialization
Champion
Affiliation
Grey Wardens
Location
Deep Roads
Appearances
Dragon Age Journeys
Martine
is a
Grey Warden
and a companion in
Dragon Age Journeys
. She is a
warrior
and uses by default a two-handed greatsword, with dual weapons as her second choice. Her
specialization
is
Champion
.
Involvement
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age Journeys
.
Martine and her mentor Feren, a
mage
and fellow Warden, journeyed to
Orzammar
to assist the
dwarves
in their millenia-long struggle against the
darkspawn
. As Feren feared going mad from the darkspawn
taint
, she adhered to
the Calling
to prevent having to force Martine to kill her and thus journeyed alone into the
Deep Roads
to slay as many darkspawn as possible.
After Feren's death, Martine commenced hunting darkspawn with renewed fervor. While in the Deep Roads she and her company were ambushed by darkspawn—the protagonist and
Ardum
managed to only rescue Martine from the onslaught. After her fallen unit was avenged, the protagonist asked Martine to help with the mystery of the
Glowing Emissary
, who Martine seemed to have some insight on. Martine wasn't very forthcoming about the Glowing Emissary and only disclosed that it is extremely dangerous and that they must warn the
King
of
Orzammar
about its presence and that it needs to be stopped. To fulfill her purpose Martine joins the protagonist's party.
After driving off the Glowing Emissary and shutting down its excavation site, Martine reveals what she knows about the mysterious Glowing Emissary and her theory on what it was after. Prior to Feren's death, she managed to acquire
Dura's Blue Flame
—a powerful artifact fabled to greatly increase the intelligence of any who use it. Martine theorized that the Glowing Emissary must have been the darkspawn who slew Feren and acquired the artifact. With Dura's Blue Flame now in the hands of the Glowing Emissary, Martine believes that the Glowing Emissary has become extremely intelligent and plans to use it to corrupt an
Old God
in order to bring about a new
Blight
.
Gear
Initial Gear
Martine's Greatsword
Martine's Armor

Keeper
's job to remember. Even the dangerous things.”

Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Dragon Age II
Heroes of Dragon Age
Dragon Age: Inquisition

Dragon Age: The Veilguard
(mentioned)
Merrill
is a Dalish
elven
mage
and
First
to the
Keeper
of the
Sabrae clan
. She is a temporary
companion
in the
Dalish Elf Origin
in
Dragon Age: Origins
and a
companion
as well as a
romance
option for either male or female
Hawke
in
Dragon Age II
.
Background
“Merrill can recite all of known elven history and navigate
the Fade
, but she has very little experience with the world or even her own people. Forced to flee north she must find help for her people, whatever the cost.
”
Merrill was born into the Alerion clan, which wandered the hills of
Nevarra
. As she was the third child of the clan with magic born to her, she was given to the Sabrae clan to be
First
to
Keeper
Marethari
at the next Arlathvhen, the gathering of Dalish clans. She was just four years old at the time.
For the story that takes place after the events of
Dragon Age: Origins
and before
Dragon Age II
, see
Merrill (short story)
.
Involvement
Dragon Age: Origins
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Merrill is a temporary party member in the
Dalish Elf Origin
. When
Tamlen
goes missing in old
Elven Ruins
, the Keeper asks Merrill to accompany
the Warden
to help find him.
Marethari
believes that Merrill's magic and knowledge of elven lore may help in the search. Tamlen cannot be recovered, but Merrill does not appear to contract the sickness that affects the Dalish elf in the course of the search, and she continues to live with the clan. If a save from
Dragon Age: Origins
with a Dalish Warden is imported to
Dragon Age II
, Merrill will express her discomfort around humans since her friend was taken away.
With a Blight rising in Ferelden, the Sabrae clan booked passage across the
Free Marches
on the
Pride of Amaranthine
, a ship that made several trips between Ferelden and the Free Marches to ferry refugees. Merrill tells
Isabela
that she was very sea-sick during the journey.
Dragon Age II
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age II
.
Act 1
Following the events surrounding the disappearance of
Tamlen
, Merrill has kept a fragment of the
Eluvian
in the old ruins to cleanse it of its taint. She studied the lore on the Eluvian, and extrapolated information from the shard.
Merrill asks Marethari for help in cleansing the shard of the taint, but the Keeper refuses.
Her efforts to construct an Eluvian—a combination of her ardent lack of interpersonal skills and her pragmatism—caused her to turn to
blood magic
, which she had learned from
Audacity
—a
pride demon
imprisoned and sundered from the Fade atop
Sundermount
. As she lacked the necessary amount of lyrium to cleanse the shard with ordinary magic, she used blood magic to amplify her power so she could succeed; this spell seemed to be utilized in the
Dalish Origin
to help
the Warden
deal with the taint. She argues with Marethari that her efforts to learn blood magic were necessary towards the restoration of the Eluvian, and ultimately the survival of the Dalish; with the Keeper publicly opposed to her efforts, the clan turns against her.
When Hawke
arrives
at the Dalish camp in
Sundermount
, she takes part in a ritual that Hawke is to perform on a mountain-top altar. In the rite, Merrill recites the song
In Uthenera
, a poem for the departed. The ritual entails the resurrection of
Flemeth
, as a portion of her
essence
is revealed to have been embedded in the amulet. Marethari explains to Hawke that Merrill had decided to leave her increasingly hostile clan to learn more about the outside world and continue her studies into restoring the Eluvian. Initially nervous when arriving in
Kirkwall
, Merrill finds
lodging
in the city's
Alienage
and joins Hawke's party. However, while staying in Kirkwall, her use of blood magic puts Merrill at odds with
Anders
and
Fenris
.
Act 2
Three years have passed since Hawke joined
the voyage
to the
Deep Roads
, and Merrill seeks Hawke's help in facing Marethari to
request
a
special dagger
to help complete the Eluvian. As they confront the Keeper, she calls upon
vir sulevanan
, the right of every Dalish to claim any property or artifact of the clan in exchange for a service. Reluctantly, Marethari requests that they slay a
Varterral
that had killed many of their hunters, as the deal for the dagger. While exploring the Varterral's lair, they run into
Pol
, who flees in fear at the sight of Merrill, straight to the Varterral itself. After killing the Varterral and returning to camp, Merrill inquires as to why Pol was so afraid of her. She discovers that Marethari had warned her clan that she might potentially bring back the darkspawn taint. Marethari tries to use this to coerce Merrill to abandon her research on the Eluvian, and return to the clan; Merrill adamantly refuses. Though Merrill honors her side of the bargain, the Keeper does not, and instead, gives it to Hawke for safekeeping, leaving Hawke with the choice to either give the Arulin'Holm to Merrill or deny her the tool.
Act 3
After another three years since the
invasion
of the Qunari, Merrill finds an
alternative
to fixing the still-inactive Eluvian, regardless whether Hawke gave the Arulin'Holm to Merrill or not. She plans to re-consult Audacity, who mentioned to her years prior that he witnessed the creation of the mirror itself during the days of Arlathan. She asks Hawke to accompany her to Sundermount, as she is concerned about the threat the demon may pose, particularly as it has been silent in her recent attempts to communicate remotely with it. It's discovered the Keeper released Audacity from its prison, and contained the demon within herself, independent of whether Hawke supports Merrill's plan or not. Marethari claims that the demon was intending to use the Eluvian as a way out of its prison and that Merrill would've been its first victim, but never explains how she came to this conclusion.
As an abomination, Marethari attacks Merrill and her companions, forcing them to kill her to survive. As Merrill mourns Marethari, Hawke can comment on the Keeper's recent actions, including informing Merrill that the Keeper "was stupid" for releasing Audacity. Afterward, the clan will confront and interrogate Hawke and Merrill about the Keeper's disappearance, eventually discovering that she is dead. If Hawke reveals the Keeper became possessed by the demon or warns the clan to back off, they will attack and force Hawke and Merrill to kill them to survive. However, if Hawke blames Merrill for Marethari's actions, the clan will only ask them to leave.
Back in the Alienage, Merrill is crushed. If she and Hawke are rivals, she bemoans that she was too "stupid and ignorant" to listen, then shatters the Eluvian. If they are on friendly terms, Merrill states that the clan and Keeper did not heed her when she was trying to help them, despite devoting several years of her life to helping end the plight of the People through the painstaking restoration of ancient elven technology that may have revitalized the People from their plight. She will consult Hawke on what should be her next course of action since the Eluvian remains inert.
Merrill pleads for
Anders
to be spared after he
destroys
the Kirkwall Chantry, and for him to assist the party and make up for his act. She willingly joins Hawke in defense of the
mages
. She can also stick with Hawke despite siding with the
Templars
, if completely loyal.
If otherwise, Merrill initially refuses to help a pro-Templar Hawke if she was not
spoken to
after an unpleasant encounter with her clan, and if Anders considers Hawke a full rival. He must also be
talked with
after fulfilling all his requests so that he can be potentially persuaded to turn against the mages; his actual fate is irrelevant.
Later, Hawke meets Merrill in the Gallows courtyard. If the Champion asks Merrill to rejoin them at this point, she can be convinced; Hawke is the only person she is very close with, a bond stronger than any she has with the Circle mages. Instead, if Hawke does not want Merrill at their side, she walks away to protect the Circle mages from the Rite of Annulment and is eventually killed in combat after her last words with Hawke.
If Merrill stands triumphant with Hawke after the battle in the Gallows, she leaves the Champion along with the others, for personal reasons, as stated by
Varric
.
Dragon Age: Inquisition
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
If Merrill survived:
The Inquisitor
can ask
Varric Tethras
about her fate. Varric will state that she remained in Kirkwall to look after the
city elves
left homeless by the fighting and continues to protect them from the fighting between the mages and the templars.
If Merrill was romanced:
Later upon asking Hawke why they're alone, they will state that Merrill had spent enough time fighting for her life with them, and they didn't want to take her away from her work with the city elves in Kirkwall/her clan.
While in the Fade,
Nightmare
will threaten Merrill in an attempt to make Hawke fearful. If Hawke is chosen to stay behind in the Fade, they will apologize to Merrill before they charge at the demon.
If Hawke leaves for
Weisshaupt Fortress
, Cassandra and Varric can have an in-party banter where Cassandra asks if Merrill will reunite with Hawke again, to which Varric responds that after Merrill reads Varric's letter, she will join up with Hawke whether they want her to or not.
While traveling into
the Crossroads
with
Morrigan
, her Eluvian can be seen in the distance.

Friendship and Rivalry
See the
approval
page for more information.
Merrill's friendship can be gained by:
Helping the mages or the elves.
Choosing options that favor blood magic or demons.
By being agreeable to her in conversation.
Rivalry with Merrill can generally be earned by contradicting the "hows" of developing a friendship with her, as mentioned above.
Romance
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age II
.
Raiments of the Dalish Pariah
Romance with Merrill is initiated once her Act 2 companion quests (
Mirror Image
,
Back From Sundermount
, and
Questioning Beliefs
) have been done. Merrill then visits the estate, followed by a heart-to-heart talk with Hawke.
Friendship Romance:
Merrill is troubled by the thought of their forbidden love; she believes the Keeper and her
clan
would object to the union because Hawke is human, since she is supposed to preserve who they are as one of the
elvhen
. But in the end, their love for one another is more important than the clan's disapproval. Merrill feels terrible as she remembers the recent turn of unfortunate events, especially Pol's death. Though she doubts that leaving her clan was the right decision, she does not regret it; she would not have known Hawke better if she stayed. She admires Hawke's leadership and ponders how differently things would be for her people if Hawke were Dalish, commenting that they would likely have a new kingdom by now.
Rivalry Romance:
Calming down, Merrill asks for Hawke's forgiveness. Though she appreciates that Hawke is keeping her from harm, Merrill warns that she is beyond redemption. She also adds that staying near her may cost the lives of the people close to Hawke. Despite their differences, Hawke stands by her.
In both variations of the romance
, if Hawke is female, Merrill expresses her feelings of wishing she was as perfect as Hawke was, and states that she admires Hawke's cleverness and beauty (as additions).
It's all right.
After the love scene, Hawke asks if Merrill could stay at the estate, her new future home so that they be with each other. Despite the controversy it might cause, she happily agrees. But Merrill does not move in completely; if Hawke checks on
Bodahn
, he suggests that
"something"
keeps her going back to her house. He often reports to Hawke of her absence upon entering the estate.
If Hawke speaks with
Isabela
sometime later after the intimate relationship with Merrill has begun, she asks if Hawke is sincere with Merrill, as they are close friends. Isabela is glad if Hawke confirms he/she has feelings for her since she notes Merrill always seemed "a little lonely." With a male Hawke, Isabela threatens to harm Hawke if he does anything nasty to Merrill. Varric also
shares his thoughts
about them being together.
Finishing
the long search
and ending
Quentin
's madness, Hawke returns home devastated. Merrill arrives and consoles Hawke, grieving silently for the loss.
During the siege of Kirkwall, Merrill expresses her concern for Hawke's safety if she is brought along on
the battle against the Qunari
.
Back before dark, I promise.
Three years later
, Hawke shares the estate with Merrill, adjusting to the
new home
she just moved in. However, she often visits her house in the Alienage to continue her construction of the Eluvian. Hawke
drops by
to assess her situation, where Merrill explains the Eluvian remains dormant, despite her best efforts. Merrill says Audacity may have knowledge of the mirror because the only creatures who would be old enough to have witnessed its forging are the denizens of the Fade. She explains that Audacity has been silent when she tried to communicate with it recently and expresses concern over this development since it's supposed to be sealed in a totem atop Sundermount. Merrill implores Hawke to aid in her
endeavor
. She also asks the Champion to kill her if something goes wrong, and she becomes possessed.
Following
their face-off
against the demon at
Pride's End
, the Dalish hunters are looking for
Marethari
and approach them with resentment. Regardless of how Hawke handles the confrontation with the clan, Merrill felt alone and rejected
as a result
. Whether the clan was killed or not, she desires to move on and asks Hawke for advice on her future course of action.
Merrill can become jealous if Hawke flirts with
Zevran
. She utters her dislike for him after
their meeting
with the
Crows
.
As Hawke helps out Anders
gather sela petrae
for his "potion", he questions their relationship. He argues that she will always give in to her weakness. Hawke contests his reproach, and Merrill retorts that Anders should examine himself more carefully instead.
Merrill can be kidnapped by the activists of
the conspiracy
set up by
Thrask
. With only Hawke in her mind during her captivity, she is grateful for her rescuer and lover.
My love...
The romance strengthens and flourishes further as they have one moment alone before
the final battle
. Expressing that the whole incident feels like a dream, Merrill is comforted by Hawke, promising that they will get through this together. Ending their conversation, they kiss amiably.
As
Varric
concludes the tale of the Champion, he says that all of the companions have parted ways, but Merrill and Hawke remain beside each other.
Alternatively, the Champion who sided with the Templars may choose to kill Merrill even if she is romanced. Merrill bitterly says before death that she did not expect such actions from Hawke.

. The change is only aesthetic; there are no other bonuses given by this armor except the runes and upgrades that have already been fitted onto it. Note that unlike Isabela and Fenris, you cannot sleep with her just to get the costume change and then break it off – you must continue the romance.

Codex entries

Trivia
Mary Kirby
wrote Merrill for
Dragon Age II
.
According to
David Gaider
, Eve Myles was last on the list of "actors I specifically asked for and got on the first try".
If you give Merrill the
Halla Carving
bought from
Master Ilen's Shop
in Act 2, it will be on display on the table beside the door to
her home
for the rest of the game.
Although Marethari warns the members of the Sabrae clan that Merrill may bring back the taint, she remains uncorrupted in her handling of the shard for several years (after successfully cleansing it of the taint with a spell amplified by blood magic), while the elves in
Witch Hunt
who came in contact with the remaining shards in the
Elven Ruins
turned into
ghouls
.
Despite claiming that Audacity would escape through the restored Eluvian, Marethari never reveals how she came to this conclusion, especially when her argument in Act 2 against this course of action was entirely different.
Morrigan
's revelation in
Witch Hunt
about the Eluvians serving as a doorway to a place "beyond this world, and beyond the Fade" call into question the validity of Marethari's claim. In
Dragon Age: Inquisition
,
Flemeth
is able to use an Eluvian to bring herself and
Kieran
to the Fade. It's also worth noting that Audacity is bound to a totem in the physical world, and is not in the Fade.
In spite of her blood magic, Merrill seems to have been taken under the wings of several of her new friends:
Varric calls her "Daisy" affectionately and is repeatedly referenced to have bribed any would-be attackers whenever she took any side alley routes around Kirkwall, and he gave her a ball of twine when she first moved to the city so she could find her way around.
Isabela calls her "Kitten" and tells her several things about being "a lady" (seduction tips, how to strut, etc.) as well as helping her with issues she isn't familiar with, because of her cultural differences with humans. Should a male Hawke enter into a relationship with Merrill, Isabela will threaten to "cut off [his] balls" if he hurts her.
In some Carver/Merrill and Carver/Isabela conversations, it is hinted that Carver may have a crush on Merrill.
Mike Laidlaw
said at PAX East 2013, that Merrill fixing the mirror was a "bad call", but did not elaborate on the context as to why.
Merrill's
vallaslin
is unique in
Dragon Age II
, though it is similar to the one she has in
Origins
. It honors
Mythal
.
Merrill mentions having fallen into the harbor when you talk to her at the docks. Apparently, the Kirkwall harbor is very deep.
The examinable pile of letters and notes in the Hawke Mansion library will have an updated text for the one mentioning Merrill watering the plants to better fit the mood between her and Hawke, if they're in a romance.
Merrill has always wanted a pet baby griffon. She says she would name it Feathers.
Not counting Sebastian, who is only available through DLC, Merrill is the only love interest in
Dragon Age II
to go by her birth name.
Merrill is currently the youngest individual known to come into their magic, at 4 years old.
In the
official cookbook
, "Merrill's blood soup" is the recipe for a rich soup with a misleading name. It is made from beetroot and topped with roasted chickpeas. It is named for its bright red color, which itself derives from the beetroot. Like Merrill and her blood magic, Devon advises the reader that they should not let the impression given by the soup put them off, and to not judge a book by its cover.
Bugs
Upon returning to the alienage after completing Long Way Home, selecting the option "I don't think so." when asked if you wish to stop by later and flirting with her during
Welcome Home
may lead to a bug with her approval for Hawke, making it impossible to gain any friendship or rivalry points with Merrill henceforth. Note: It has also been noted that this bug randomly occurs even if you select the "I Will." option and later flirt with her. You'll know it's not working because you'll gain no friendship after all friendly and flirt dialogue options have been concluded in Welcome Home. If this does happen, simply reload and do not select the flirt option when chatting with Merrill in Welcome Home. This initial flirt is fortunately not required to begin a romance.
If the sibling either joined the Wardens or died during the expedition, the quest "Mourning" was supposed to take place.
However, it has since been cut from the game, but may reappear if the game has not been patched.
There have been some cases where, during the sex scene between her and Hawke, some graphical issues occur. Such reports claim that the two characters abruptly show up standing idly at the entrance to the Hawke estate, both in their intimate attire. The dialogue wheel, however, is still present and choosing any option will promptly fix the bug, placing the characters in their proper place and the scene will proceed normally.
xbox360
During Act 3, the Questioning Beliefs involving Merrill's reaction to the end of A New Path will occur, even before you completed the quest, where she is upset by the death of her clan's Keeper. It can be worked around, but is messing with the chronological order of the related quests. (2, 1, 3).
In Act 3, when initiating A New Path, it seems that if you are on a path of rivalry with Merrill, she will mention you keeping the arulin'holm from her, even if you gave it to her.
If her clan is wiped out after her personal quest A New Path, she is stuck to your party, being locked forever. This can be fixed by either taking another quest where a party member is locked in, such as
Sebastian
's
Faith
, or by returning home to the Hawke estate. Both methods will remove all current party members, restoring Merrill's unlocked status.
With patch 1.03, some of the Act 3 romance scenes may not trigger properly, but the flow will proceed as planned.
If she is wearing Raiments of the Dalish Pariah and
is kidnapped
in Act 3, she will be seen wearing Vestments of the First instead.
You can complete the quest "Questioning Beliefs" in Act 3 by accessing the console and manually maxing Merrill's Friendship meter.

“I haven't taken my Joining yet, but I look forward to fighting at your side.”
Character
Mhairi
Title
Ser
Warden-Recruit
Race
Human
Gender
Female
Class
Warrior
Specialization
Champion

Grey Wardens
Quests

Alix Wilton Regan
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
Heroes of Dragon Age
Ser
Mhairi
is a
warrior
and
Grey Warden
recruit. She is a
companion
in
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
.
Background
Mhairi is a knight from Dragon's Peak.
When Mhairi was a child, she was enraptured by tales of kings and nobles. All she wanted was to be like them, to fight for what she believed. Betrothed to a trader when she turned sixteen, she saw the life that lay ahead of her and enlisted in the army instead. Her fierce loyalty and devotion to
Ferelden
earned her the love and respect of her commanders and brothers-in-arms and she was eventually knighted.
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Mhairi fought in the
Battle of Ostagar
.
Loghain
's
betrayal
of King
Cailan
and the King's death hit her hard, and when she learned of the two
Grey Wardens
who quelled the
Blight
almost single-handedly, she vowed to serve the Wardens. When the call came for volunteers to help rebuild the order in the
Arling of Amaranthine
, Mhairi jumped at the chance to join.
Involvement
Mhairi was serving at
Vigil's Keep
under the
Orlesian
Grey Wardens who were assigned to Ferelden to help re-establish the Order there.
Oghren
was present at the Vigil at the same time and Mhairi found herself irritated by his cavalier attitude. She was assigned to escort the new
Warden-Commander
back to Vigil's Keep. Upon returning to discover the fortress is under assault, Mhairi assists the Warden-Commander in clearing the Keep of
darkspawn
. If engaged in conversation, Mhairi will mention that she hasn't gone through her
Joining
yet.
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
.
During the
defense of Vigil's Keep
, Mhairi and the Warden-Commander encounter her friend, Rowland, a fellow knight from
Denerim
who is also a Warden-Recruit. Rowland has already been fatally wounded however and dies from the darkspawn
taint
. Mhairi swears to avenge his death.
Despite her enthusiasm for the Wardens, Mhairi does not survive the Joining ritual. Fellow Warden Nolan, who undertook the Joining a week after her, wrote down her name in his list of Wardens who didn't survive the Joining as a way to honor and remember her.
The Warden can get an
approval
increase or decrease of +13 or -25 with her before the first Joining, without the use of
gifts
. The Warden only has to tell her that they will kill the darkspawn anyway and show concern for the wounded Rowland, or threaten to leave her behind and vote for an early death of Rowland. Her approval can be increased further using gifts imported with a character from
Origins
. Unlike other party members, her approval bonus for multiple gifts does not appear to decrease but high approval also does not give her any
attribute
bonus.
Quests
The Assault on Vigil's Keep

companions
, her helmet remains present in dialog cutscenes if equipped.
Mhairi is the only temporary companion whose
approval
increases and decreases and who has a character trailer.

:
Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening
.

“Change is coming to the world. Many fear change and will fight it with every fiber of their being. But sometimes, change is what they need the most. Sometimes, change is what sets them free.”
Character
